Finally, we propose possible future trends for vision-radar fusion to enlighten future research. Regularly updated summary can be found at: https://github.com/ApoorvRoboticist/Vision-RADAR-Fusion-BEV-Survey
abstract_zh: 由于构建自主机器人感知系统的趋势需求，传感器融合吸引了研究人员和工程师的广泛关注，以充分利用跨模态信息。然而，为了大规模构建机器人平台，我们还需要强调自主机器人平台的启动成本。摄像机和雷达本质上包含互补的感知信息，具有大规模开发自主机器人平台的潜力。然而，与激光雷达与视觉融合的工作相比，雷达与视觉融合的工作有限。在本文中，我们通过对 BEV 目标检测系统的视觉雷达融合方法的调查来解决这一差距。首先，我们了解背景信息，即物体检测任务、传感器选择、传感器设置、基准数据集和机器人感知系统的评估指标。随后，我们将介绍每种模态（相机和雷达）的数据表示，然后详细介绍基于子组的传感器融合技术，即早期融合、深度融合和后期融合，以便轻松了解每种方法的优缺点。最后，我们提出了视觉雷达融合的未来可能趋势，以启发未来的研究。定期更新的摘要可以在以下位置找到：https://github.com/ApoorvRoboticist/Vision-RADAR-Fusion-BEV-Survey

AI.tldr: 综述视觉-雷达融合在机器人BEV目标检测中的方法
AI.motivation: 自主机器人感知系统需多传感器融合，但视觉与雷达融合研究较少，需填补该领域研究空白
AI.method: 系统梳理传感器选择、数据表示、融合技术分类（早融合/深融合/晚融合）及评估指标
AI.result: 总结现有方法优缺点，提出未来研究方向如跨模态学习与算法优化
AI.conclusion: 视觉-雷达融合具成本优势，当前研究需突破数据对齐与特征融合瓶颈

authors_text: Chulin Xie,De-An Huang,Wenda Chu,Daguang Xu,Chaowei Xiao,Bo Li,Anima Anandkumar
title: PerAda: Parameter-Efficient Federated Learning Personalization with Generalization Guarantees
title_zh: PerAda：具有泛化保证的参数高效联合学习个性化
abstract: Personalized Federated Learning (pFL) has emerged as a promising solution to tackle data heterogeneity across clients in FL. However, existing pFL methods either (1) introduce high communication and computation costs or (2) overfit to local data, which can be limited in scope, and are vulnerable to evolved test samples with natural shifts. In this paper, we propose PerAda, a parameter-efficient pFL framework that reduces communication and computational costs and exhibits superior generalization performance, especially under test-time distribution shifts. PerAda reduces the costs by leveraging the power of pretrained models and only updates and communicates a small number of additional parameters from adapters. PerAda has good generalization since it regularizes each client's personalized adapter with a global adapter, while the global adapter uses knowledge distillation to aggregate generalized information from all clients. Theoretically, we provide generalization bounds to explain why PerAda improves generalization, and we prove its convergence to stationary points under non-convex settings. Empirically, PerAda demonstrates competitive personalized performance (+4.85% on CheXpert) and enables better out-of-distribution generalization (+5.23% on CIFAR-10-C) on different datasets across natural and medical domains compared with baselines, while only updating 12.6% of parameters per model based on the adapter. Our code is available at https://github.com/NVlabs/PerAda.
summary: Personalized Federated Learning (pFL) has emerged as a promising solution to tackle data heterogeneity across clients in FL. However, existing pFL methods either (1) introduce high communication and computation costs or (2) overfit to local data, which can be limited in scope, and are vulnerable to evolved test samples with natural shifts. In this paper, we propose PerAda, a parameter-efficient pFL framework that reduces communication and computational costs and exhibits superior generalization performance, especially under test-time distribution shifts. PerAda reduces the costs by leveraging the power of pretrained models and only updates and communicates a small number of additional parameters from adapters. PerAda has good generalization since it regularizes each client's personalized adapter with a global adapter, while the global adapter uses knowledge distillation to aggregate generalized information from all clients. Theoretically, we provide generalization bounds to explain why PerAda improves generalization, and we prove its convergence to stationary points under non-convex settings. Empirically, PerAda demonstrates competitive personalized performance (+4.85% on CheXpert) and enables better out-of-distribution generalization (+5.23% on CIFAR-10-C) on different datasets across natural and medical domains compared with baselines, while only updating 12.6% of parameters per model based on the adapter. Our code is available at https://github.com/NVlabs/PerAda.
abstract_zh: 个性化联合学习 (pFL) 已成为解决 FL 客户间数据异构性的有前景的解决方案。然而，现有的 pFL 方法要么 (1) 引入较高的通信和计算成本，要么 (2) 过度拟合本地数据，其范围有限，并且容易受到具有自然变化的演化测试样本的影响。在本文中，我们提出了 PerAda，一种参数高效的 p​​FL 框架，可以减少通信和计算成本，并表现出卓越的泛化性能，尤其是在测试时间分布变化的情况下。 PerAda 通过利用预训练模型的强大功能来降低成本，并且仅更新和传​​递来自适配器的少量附加参数。 PerAda 具有良好的泛化性，因为它用全局适配器规范每个客户端的个性化适配器，而全局适配器使用知识蒸馏来聚合来自所有客户端的通用信息。理论上，我们提供泛化界限来解释为什么 PerAda 提高了泛化能力，并且我们证明了它在非凸设置下收敛到驻点。根据经验，与基线相比，PerAda 在自然和医学领域的不同数据集上展示了有竞争力的个性化性能（在 CheXpert 上+4.85%），并实现了更好的分布外泛化（在 CIFAR-10-C 上+5.23%），同时基于适配器仅更新每个模型 12.6% 的参数。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/NVlabs/PerAda 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PerAda框架，在联邦学习中实现参数高效的个性化，同时保证泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 现有个性化联邦学习方法存在通信计算成本高或过度拟合本地数据的问题，且难以应对测试时的分布变化。
AI.method: 利用预训练模型仅更新少量适配器参数，通过全局适配器与知识蒸馏聚合通用信息，理论证明泛化界并保证非凸场景收敛。
AI.result: 在CheXpert数据集上提升4.85%，CIFAR-10-C上提升5.23%，每模型仅更新12.6%参数。
AI.conclusion: PerAda通过适配器机制有效平衡个性化与泛化，为高效联邦学习提供理论支撑与实践方案。

title: Tensor Networks or Decision Diagrams? Guidelines for Classical Quantum Circuit Simulation
title_zh: 张量网络还是决策图？经典量子电路仿真指南
abstract: Classically simulating quantum circuits is crucial when developing or testing quantum algorithms. Due to the underlying exponential complexity, efficient data structures are key for performing such simulations. To this end, tensor networks and decision diagrams have independently been developed with differing perspectives, terminologies, and backgrounds in mind. Although this left designers with two complementary data structures for quantum circuit simulation, thus far it remains unclear which one is the better choice for a given use case. In this work, we (1) consider how these techniques approach classical quantum circuit simulation, and (2) examine their (dis)similarities with regard to their most applicable abstraction level, the desired simulation output, the impact of the computation order, and the ease of distributing the workload. As a result, we provide guidelines for when to better use tensor networks and when to better use decision diagrams in classical quantum circuit simulation.
summary: Classically simulating quantum circuits is crucial when developing or testing quantum algorithms. Due to the underlying exponential complexity, efficient data structures are key for performing such simulations. To this end, tensor networks and decision diagrams have independently been developed with differing perspectives, terminologies, and backgrounds in mind. Although this left designers with two complementary data structures for quantum circuit simulation, thus far it remains unclear which one is the better choice for a given use case. In this work, we (1) consider how these techniques approach classical quantum circuit simulation, and (2) examine their (dis)similarities with regard to their most applicable abstraction level, the desired simulation output, the impact of the computation order, and the ease of distributing the workload. As a result, we provide guidelines for when to better use tensor networks and when to better use decision diagrams in classical quantum circuit simulation.
abstract_zh: 在开发或测试量子算法时，经典模拟量子电路至关重要。由于潜在的指数复杂性，有效的数据结构是执行此类模拟的关键。为此，张量网络和决策图是根据不同的观点、术语和背景独立开发的。尽管这为设计人员留下了两种用于量子电路模拟的互补数据结构，但迄今为止仍不清楚哪一种是给定用例的更好选择。在这项工作中，我们（1）考虑这些技术如何实现经典量子电路模拟，（2）检查它们在最适用的抽象级别、所需的模拟输出、计算顺序的影响以及分配工作负载的难易程度方面的相似性。因此，我们提供了在经典量子电路模拟中何时更好地使用张量网络以及何时更好地使用决策图的指南。

AI.tldr: 本文分析了张量网络与决策图在经典量子电路模拟中的适用场景，提供选择指南。
AI.motivation: 量子电路模拟因指数复杂性需高效数据结构，但张量网络与决策图的适用性缺乏明确对比。
AI.method: 通过比较两者在抽象层级、输出需求、计算顺序影响及并行化难度等方面的差异进行分析。
AI.result: 明确了张量网络与决策图在不同场景下的优劣，给出具体使用建议。
AI.conclusion: 两种方法各有适用场景，需根据模拟目标与约束条件选择合适技术路径。

search_text: Tensor Networks or Decision Diagrams? Guidelines for Classical Quantum Circuit Simulation 张量网络还是决策图？经典量子电路仿真指南 Classically simulating quantum circuits is crucial when developing or testing quantum algorithms. Due to the underlying exponential complexity, efficient data structures are key for performing such simulations. To this end, tensor networks and decision diagrams have independently been developed with differing perspectives, terminologies, and backgrounds in mind. Although this left designers with two complementary data structures for quantum circuit simulation, thus far it remains unclear which one is the better choice for a given use case. In this work, we (1) consider how these techniques approach classical quantum circuit simulation, and (2) examine their (dis)similarities with regard to their most applicable abstraction level, the desired simulation output, the impact of the computation order, and the ease of distributing the workload. As a result, we provide guidelines for when to better use tensor networks and when to better use decision diagrams in classical quantum circuit simulation. 在开发或测试量子算法时，经典模拟量子电路至关重要。由于潜在的指数复杂性，有效的数据结构是执行此类模拟的关键。为此，张量网络和决策图是根据不同的观点、术语和背景独立开发的。尽管这为设计人员留下了两种用于量子电路模拟的互补数据结构，但迄今为止仍不清楚哪一种是给定用例的更好选择。在这项工作中，我们（1）考虑这些技术如何实现经典量子电路模拟，（2）检查它们在最适用的抽象级别、所需的模拟输出、计算顺序的影响以及分配工作负载的难易程度方面的相似性。因此，我们提供了在经典量子电路模拟中何时更好地使用张量网络以及何时更好地使用决策图的指南。 本文分析了张量网络与决策图在经典量子电路模拟中的适用场景，提供选择指南。 Lukas Burgholzer,Alexander Ploier,Robert Wille quant-ph,cs.DS,cs.ET

title: Self-Emotion-Mediated Exploration in Artificial Intelligence Mirrors: Findings from Cognitive Psychology
title_zh: 人工智能镜子中自我情绪介导的探索：认知心理学的发现
abstract: Background: Exploration of the physical environment is an indispensable precursor to information acquisition and knowledge consolidation for living organisms. Yet, current artificial intelligence models lack these autonomy capabilities during training, hindering their adaptability. This work proposes a learning framework for artificial agents to obtain an intrinsic exploratory drive, based on epistemic and achievement emotions triggered during data observation. Methods: This study proposes a dual-module reinforcement framework, where data analysis scores dictate pride or surprise, in accordance with psychological studies on humans. A correlation between these states and exploration is then optimized for agents to meet their learning goals. Results: Causal relationships between states and exploration are demonstrated by the majority of agents. A 15.4\% mean increase is noted for surprise, with a 2.8\% mean decrease for pride. Resulting correlations of $ρ_{surprise}=0.461$ and $ρ_{pride}=-0.237$ are obtained, mirroring previously reported human behavior. Conclusions: These findings lead to the conclusion that bio-inspiration for AI development can be of great use. This can incur benefits typically found in living beings, such as autonomy. Further, it empirically shows how AI methodologies can corroborate human behavioral findings, showcasing major interdisciplinary importance. Ramifications are discussed.

Yet, current artificial intelligence models lack these autonomy capabilities during training, hindering their adaptability. This work proposes a learning framework for artificial agents to obtain an intrinsic exploratory drive, based on epistemic and achievement emotions triggered during data observation. Methods: This study proposes a dual-module reinforcement framework, where data analysis scores dictate pride or surprise, in accordance with psychological studies on humans. A correlation between these states and exploration is then optimized for agents to meet their learning goals. Results: Causal relationships between states and exploration are demonstrated by the majority of agents. A 15.4\% mean increase is noted for surprise, with a 2.8\% mean decrease for pride. Resulting correlations of $ρ_{surprise}=0.461$ and $ρ_{pride}=-0.237$ are obtained, mirroring previously reported human behavior. Conclusions: These findings lead to the conclusion that bio-inspiration for AI development can be of great use. This can incur benefits typically found in living beings, such as autonomy. Further, it empirically shows how AI methodologies can corroborate human behavioral findings, showcasing major interdisciplinary importance. Ramifications are discussed.
abstract_zh: 背景：对物理环境的探索是生物体信息获取和知识巩固不可或缺的前提。然而，当前的人工智能模型在训练过程中缺乏这些自主能力，从而阻碍了它们的适应性。这项工作提出了一个学习框架，使人工智能体能够基于数据观察过程中触发的认知和成就情绪来获得内在的探索动力。方法：本研究提出了一个双模块强化框架，根据人类心理学研究，数据分析分数决定自豪或惊喜。然后优化这些状态和探索之间的相关性，以实现代理的学习目标。结果：大多数智能体都证明了状态和探索之间的因果关系。惊喜平均增加 15.4%，自豪平均减少 2.8%。获得 $ρ_{surprise}=0.461$ 和 $ρ_{pride}=-0.237$ 的相关性，反映了先前报告的人类行为。结论：这些发现得出这样的结论：人工智能开发的生物灵感非常有用。这可以带来通常在生物中发现的好处，例如自主性。此外，它还凭经验展示了人工智能方法如何证实人类行为发现，展示了重大的跨学科重要性。讨论了后果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于人类情绪机制的AI探索框架，通过模拟认知心理学中的惊奇与自豪情绪，增强人工智能代理的自主探索能力。
AI.motivation: 现有AI模型缺乏自主探索能力，限制了其适应性。生物启发式方法可赋予AI类似生命的自主性，同时验证人类行为研究的理论价值。
AI.method: 构建双模块强化学习框架，根据数据观测结果生成情绪信号（惊奇/自豪），通过优化情绪与探索行为的关联性实现自主学习。
AI.result: 实验显示惊奇情绪使探索行为提升15.4%（ρ=0.461），自豪情绪降低2.8%（ρ=-0.237），与人类认知心理学研究结果一致。
AI.conclusion: 生物启发式AI框架能有效提升自主性，证明AI方法可验证人类行为理论，具有跨学科研究价值。

authors_text: Weina Jin,Jianyu Fan,Diane Gromala,Philippe Pasquier,Ghassan Hamarneh
title: Invisible Users: Uncovering End-Users' Requirements for Explainable AI via Explanation Forms and Goals
title_zh: 隐形用户：通过解释形式和目标揭示最终用户对可解释人工智能的需求
comment: EUCA dataset, code, and appendix are at: https://weinajin.github.io/euca. An earlier longer version of the EUCA study (with EUCA prototyping framework) is at: arXiv:2102.02437. The followup paper applying the EUCA study findings is at: arXiv:2208.08739
comments: EUCA dataset, code, and appendix are at: https://weinajin.github.io/euca. An earlier longer version of the EUCA study (with EUCA prototyping framework) is at: arXiv:2102.02437. The followup paper applying the EUCA study findings is at: arXiv:2208.08739
abstract: Non-technical end-users are silent and invisible users of the state-of-the-art explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) technologies. Their demands and requirements for AI explainability are not incorporated into the design and evaluation of XAI techniques, which are developed to explain the rationales of AI decisions to end-users and assist their critical decisions. This makes XAI techniques ineffective or even harmful in high-stakes applications, such as healthcare, criminal justice, finance, and autonomous driving systems. To systematically understand end-users' requirements to support the technical development of XAI, we conducted the EUCA user study with 32 layperson participants in four AI-assisted critical tasks. The study identified comprehensive user requirements for feature-, example-, and rule-based XAI techniques (manifested by the end-user-friendly explanation forms) and XAI evaluation objectives (manifested by the explanation goals), which were shown to be helpful to directly inspire the proposal of new XAI algorithms and evaluation metrics. The EUCA study findings, the identified explanation forms and goals for technical specification, and the EUCA study dataset support the design and evaluation of end-user-centered XAI techniques for accessible, safe, and accountable AI.
summary: Non-technical end-users are silent and invisible users of the state-of-the-art explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) technologies. Their demands and requirements for AI explainability are not incorporated into the design and evaluation of XAI techniques, which are developed to explain the rationales of AI decisions to end-users and assist their critical decisions. This makes XAI techniques ineffective or even harmful in high-stakes applications, such as healthcare, criminal justice, finance, and autonomous driving systems. To systematically understand end-users' requirements to support the technical development of XAI, we conducted the EUCA user study with 32 layperson participants in four AI-assisted critical tasks. The study identified comprehensive user requirements for feature-, example-, and rule-based XAI techniques (manifested by the end-user-friendly explanation forms) and XAI evaluation objectives (manifested by the explanation goals), which were shown to be helpful to directly inspire the proposal of new XAI algorithms and evaluation metrics. The EUCA study findings, the identified explanation forms and goals for technical specification, and the EUCA study dataset support the design and evaluation of end-user-centered XAI techniques for accessible, safe, and accountable AI.
abstract_zh: 非技术最终用户是最先进的可解释人工智能 (XAI) 技术的沉默且隐形的用户。他们对人工智能可解释性的需求和要求并未纳入 XAI 技术的设计和评估中，而 XAI 技术的开发是为了向最终用户解释人工智能决策的基本原理并协助他们做出关键决策。这使得 XAI 技术在医疗保健、刑事司法、金融和自动驾驶系统等高风险应用中无效甚至有害。为了系统地了解最终用户支持 XAI 技术开发的需求，我们对 32 名外行参与者进行了 EUCA 用户研究，涉及四项人工智能辅助的关键任务。该研究确定了基于特征、示例和规则的 XAI 技术（通过最终用户友好的解释形式体现）和 XAI 评估目标（通过解释目标体现）的综合用户需求，这有助于直接激发新 XAI 算法和评估指标的提出。 EUCA 研究结果、确定的技术规范解释形式和目标以及 EUCA 研究数据集支持以最终用户为中心的 XAI 技术的设计和评估，以实现可访问、安全和负责任的 AI。

AI.tldr: 通过分析非技术用户对可解释AI的需求，提出以用户为中心的XAI设计与评估方法。
AI.motivation: 现有XAI技术忽视非技术用户需求，导致高风险场景下效果不佳或有害，需系统研究用户需求以改进技术。
AI.method: 通过EUCA用户研究，调查32名普通用户在四个关键AI任务中的需求，识别解释形式与目标。
AI.result: 识别出用户对特征、示例、规则基于XAI技术的解释形式及评估目标，为新算法和评估指标提供依据。
AI.conclusion: 研究结果支持设计以用户为中心的XAI技术，推动更安全、可问责的AI发展。

conclusion: 研究结果支持设计以用户为中心的XAI技术，推动更安全、可问责的AI发展。
search_text: Invisible Users: Uncovering End-Users' Requirements for Explainable AI via Explanation Forms and Goals 隐形用户：通过解释形式和目标揭示最终用户对可解释人工智能的需求 Non-technical end-users are silent and invisible users of the state-of-the-art explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) technologies. Their demands and requirements for AI explainability are not incorporated into the design and evaluation of XAI techniques, which are developed to explain the rationales of AI decisions to end-users and assist their critical decisions. This makes XAI techniques ineffective or even harmful in high-stakes applications, such as healthcare, criminal justice, finance, and autonomous driving systems. To systematically understand end-users' requirements to support the technical development of XAI, we conducted the EUCA user study with 32 layperson participants in four AI-assisted critical tasks. The study identified comprehensive user requirements for feature-, example-, and rule-based XAI techniques (manifested by the end-user-friendly explanation forms) and XAI evaluation objectives (manifested by the explanation goals), which were shown to be helpful to directly inspire the proposal of new XAI algorithms and evaluation metrics. The EUCA study findings, the identified explanation forms and goals for technical specification, and the EUCA study dataset support the design and evaluation of end-user-centered XAI techniques for accessible, safe, and accountable AI. 非技术最终用户是最先进的可解释人工智能 (XAI) 技术的沉默且隐形的用户。他们对人工智能可解释性的需求和要求并未纳入 XAI 技术的设计和评估中，而 XAI 技术的开发是为了向最终用户解释人工智能决策的基本原理并协助他们做出关键决策。这使得 XAI 技术在医疗保健、刑事司法、金融和自动驾驶系统等高风险应用中无效甚至有害。为了系统地了解最终用户支持 XAI 技术开发的需求，我们对 32 名外行参与者进行了 EUCA 用户研究，涉及四项人工智能辅助的关键任务。该研究确定了基于特征、示例和规则的 XAI 技术（通过最终用户友好的解释形式体现）和 XAI 评估目标（通过解释目标体现）的综合用户需求，这有助于直接激发新 XAI 算法和评估指标的提出。 EUCA 研究结果、确定的技术规范解释形式和目标以及 EUCA 研究数据集支持以最终用户为中心的 XAI 技术的设计和评估，以实现可访问、安全和负责任的 AI。 通过分析非技术用户对可解释AI的需求，提出以用户为中心的XAI设计与评估方法。 Weina Jin,Jianyu Fan,Diane Gromala,Philippe Pasquier,Ghassan Hamarneh cs.HC,cs.AI

authors_text: Hyunsu Kim,Gayoung Lee,Yunjey Choi,Jin-Hwa Kim,Jun-Yan Zhu
title: 3D-aware Blending with Generative NeRFs
title_zh: 使用生成式 NeRF 进行 3D 感知混合
abstract: Image blending aims to combine multiple images seamlessly. It remains challenging for existing 2D-based methods, especially when input images are misaligned due to differences in 3D camera poses and object shapes. To tackle these issues, we propose a 3D-aware blending method using generative Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF), including two key components: 3D-aware alignment and 3D-aware blending. For 3D-aware alignment, we first estimate the camera pose of the reference image with respect to generative NeRFs and then perform 3D local alignment for each part. To further leverage 3D information of the generative NeRF, we propose 3D-aware blending that directly blends images on the NeRF's latent representation space, rather than raw pixel space. Collectively, our method outperforms existing 2D baselines, as validated by extensive quantitative and qualitative evaluations with FFHQ and AFHQ-Cat.
summary: Image blending aims to combine multiple images seamlessly. It remains challenging for existing 2D-based methods, especially when input images are misaligned due to differences in 3D camera poses and object shapes. To tackle these issues, we propose a 3D-aware blending method using generative Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF), including two key components: 3D-aware alignment and 3D-aware blending. For 3D-aware alignment, we first estimate the camera pose of the reference image with respect to generative NeRFs and then perform 3D local alignment for each part. To further leverage 3D information of the generative NeRF, we propose 3D-aware blending that directly blends images on the NeRF's latent representation space, rather than raw pixel space. Collectively, our method outperforms existing 2D baselines, as validated by extensive quantitative and qualitative evaluations with FFHQ and AFHQ-Cat.
abstract_zh: 图像混合的目的是无缝地组合多个图像。对于现有的基于 2D 的方法来说仍然具有挑战性，特别是当输入图像由于 3D 相机姿势和物体形状的差异而未对齐时。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了一种使用生成神经辐射场（NeRF）的 3D 感知混合方法，包括两个关键组件：3D 感知对齐和 3D 感知混合。对于 3D 感知对齐，我们首先估计参考图像相对于生成 NeRF 的相机位姿，然后对每个部分执行 3D 局部对齐。为了进一步利用生成 NeRF 的 3D 信息，我们提出了 3D 感知混合，直接在 NeRF 的潜在表示空间而不是原始像素空间上混合图像。总的来说，我们的方法优于现有的 2D 基线，这一点通过 FFHQ 和 AFHQ-Cat 的广泛定量和定性评估得到验证。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于生成神经辐射场（NeRF）的3D感知图像融合方法，解决传统2D方法在处理3D相机姿态和物体形状差异导致的图像对齐问题。
AI.motivation: 现有2D图像融合方法难以处理因3D相机姿态差异和物体形状变化导致的输入图像错位问题，需利用3D信息提升融合效果。
AI.method: 方法包含两个核心组件：1）通过估计参考图像与生成NeRF的相机姿态并进行3D局部对齐；2）在NeRF的潜在表示空间而非像素空间直接融合图像。
AI.result: 在FFHQ和AFHQ-Cat数据集上，该方法在定量和定性评估中均优于现有2D基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 基于生成NeRF的3D感知融合方法有效解决了传统2D方法的局限性，为复杂场景下的图像融合提供了新思路。

title: Breaking the Curse of Multiagency: Provably Efficient Decentralized Multi-Agent RL with Function Approximation
title_zh: 打破多代理的魔咒：具有函数逼近的可证明高效的去中心化多代理强化学习
abstract: A unique challenge in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) is the curse of multiagency, where the description length of the game as well as the complexity of many existing learning algorithms scale exponentially with the number of agents. While recent works successfully address this challenge under the model of tabular Markov Games, their mechanisms critically rely on the number of states being finite and small, and do not extend to practical scenarios with enormous state spaces where function approximation must be used to approximate value functions or policies. This paper presents the first line of MARL algorithms that provably resolve the curse of multiagency under function approximation. We design a new decentralized algorithm -- V-Learning with Policy Replay, which gives the first polynomial sample complexity results for learning approximate Coarse Correlated Equilibria (CCEs) of Markov Games under decentralized linear function approximation. Our algorithm always outputs Markov CCEs, and achieves an optimal rate of $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(ε^{-2})$ for finding $ε$-optimal solutions. Also, when restricted to the tabular case, our result improves over the current best decentralized result $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(ε^{-3})$ for finding Markov CCEs. We further present an alternative algorithm -- Decentralized Optimistic Policy Mirror Descent, which finds policy-class-restricted CCEs using a polynomial number of samples. In exchange for learning a weaker version of CCEs, this algorithm applies to a wider range of problems under generic function approximation, such as linear quadratic games and MARL problems with low ''marginal'' Eluder dimension.
summary: A unique challenge in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) is the curse of multiagency, where the description length of the game as well as the complexity of many existing learning algorithms scale exponentially with the number of agents. While recent works successfully address this challenge under the model of tabular Markov Games, their mechanisms critically rely on the number of states being finite and small, and do not extend to practical scenarios with enormous state spaces where function approximation must be used to approximate value functions or policies. This paper presents the first line of MARL algorithms that provably resolve the curse of multiagency under function approximation. We design a new decentralized algorithm -- V-Learning with Policy Replay, which gives the first polynomial sample complexity results for learning approximate Coarse Correlated Equilibria (CCEs) of Markov Games under decentralized linear function approximation. Our algorithm always outputs Markov CCEs, and achieves an optimal rate of $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(ε^{-2})$ for finding $ε$-optimal solutions. Also, when restricted to the tabular case, our result improves over the current best decentralized result $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(ε^{-3})$ for finding Markov CCEs. We further present an alternative algorithm -- Decentralized Optimistic Policy Mirror Descent, which finds policy-class-restricted CCEs using a polynomial number of samples. In exchange for learning a weaker version of CCEs, this algorithm applies to a wider range of problems under generic function approximation, such as linear quadratic games and MARL problems with low ''marginal'' Eluder dimension.
abstract_zh: 多代理强化学习（MARL）中的一个独特挑战是多代理的诅咒，其中游戏的描述长度以及许多现有学习算法的复杂性随着代理的数量呈指数级增长。虽然最近的工作在表格马尔可夫博弈模型下成功地解决了这一挑战，但它们的机制严重依赖于有限且小的状态数量，并且不能扩展到具有巨大状态空间的实际场景，在这种场景中必须使用函数逼近来逼近价值函数或策略。本文提出了第一行 MARL 算法，可证明解决函数逼近下多代理的诅咒。我们设计了一种新的去中心化算法——V-Learning with Policy Replay，它给出了在去中心化线性函数逼近下学习马尔可夫博弈的粗相关均衡（CCE）的第一个多项式样本复杂度结果。我们的算法始终输出马尔可夫 CCE，并达到 $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(ε^{-2})$ 的最佳速率，以找到 $ε$ 最优解。此外，当限制于表格情况时，我们的结果比当前寻找马尔可夫 CCE 的最佳去中心化结果 $\widetilde{\mathcal{O}}(ε^{-3})$ 有所改进。我们进一步提出了一种替代算法——去中心化乐观策略镜像下降，它使用多项式数量的样本找到策略类限制的 CCE。作为学习 CCE 较弱版本的交换，该算法适用于泛函数逼近下更广泛的问题，例如线性二次博弈和具有低“边缘”Eluder 维数的 MARL 问题。

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种去中心化多智能体强化学习算法，解决大规模状态空间下的多智能体博弈中的多机构诅咒问题。
AI.motivation: 现有MARL算法在处理大规模状态空间时面临复杂度指数级增长的挑战，且依赖有限状态假设，难以实际应用。
AI.method: 设计V-Learning with Policy Replay算法及Decentralized Optimistic Policy Mirror Descent算法，结合函数逼近实现去中心化学习。
AI.result: 首次实现多项式样本复杂度学习近似粗略相关均衡，ε-最优解速率达Õ(ε⁻²)，并扩展至线性二次博弈等更广泛场景。
AI.conclusion: 提出的算法有效突破多机构诅咒限制，在函数逼近框架下实现高效去中心化多智能体学习。

authors_text: Haoyu Lu,Yuqi Huo,Guoxing Yang,Zhiwu Lu,Wei Zhan,Masayoshi Tomizuka,Mingyu Ding
title: UniAdapter: Unified Parameter-Efficient Transfer Learning for Cross-modal Modeling
title_zh: UniAdapter：用于跨模态建模的统一参数高效迁移学习
abstract: Large-scale vision-language pre-trained models have shown promising transferability to various downstream tasks. As the size of these foundation models and the number of downstream tasks grow, the standard full fine-tuning paradigm becomes unsustainable due to heavy computational and storage costs. This paper proposes UniAdapter, which unifies unimodal and multimodal adapters for parameter-efficient cross-modal adaptation on pre-trained vision-language models. Specifically, adapters are distributed to different modalities and their interactions, with the total number of tunable parameters reduced by partial weight sharing. The unified and knowledge-sharing design enables powerful cross-modal representations that can benefit various downstream tasks, requiring only 1.0%-2.0% tunable parameters of the pre-trained model. Extensive experiments on 6 cross-modal downstream benchmarks (including video-text retrieval, image-text retrieval, VideoQA, and VQA) show that in most cases, UniAdapter not only outperforms the state-of-the-arts, but even beats the full fine-tuning strategy. Particularly, on the MSRVTT retrieval task, UniAdapter achieves 49.7% recall@1 with 2.2% model parameters, outperforming the latest competitors by 2.0%. The code and models are available at https://github.com/RERV/UniAdapter.
summary: Large-scale vision-language pre-trained models have shown promising transferability to various downstream tasks. As the size of these foundation models and the number of downstream tasks grow, the standard full fine-tuning paradigm becomes unsustainable due to heavy computational and storage costs. This paper proposes UniAdapter, which unifies unimodal and multimodal adapters for parameter-efficient cross-modal adaptation on pre-trained vision-language models. Specifically, adapters are distributed to different modalities and their interactions, with the total number of tunable parameters reduced by partial weight sharing. The unified and knowledge-sharing design enables powerful cross-modal representations that can benefit various downstream tasks, requiring only 1.0%-2.0% tunable parameters of the pre-trained model. Extensive experiments on 6 cross-modal downstream benchmarks (including video-text retrieval, image-text retrieval, VideoQA, and VQA) show that in most cases, UniAdapter not only outperforms the state-of-the-arts, but even beats the full fine-tuning strategy. Particularly, on the MSRVTT retrieval task, UniAdapter achieves 49.7% recall@1 with 2.2% model parameters, outperforming the latest competitors by 2.0%. The code and models are available at https://github.com/RERV/UniAdapter.
abstract_zh: 大规模视觉语言预训练模型已显示出向各种下游任务的可迁移性。随着这些基础模型的规模和下游任务数量的增长，由于计算和存储成本高昂，标准的完全微调范式变得不可持续。本文提出了 UniAdapter，它统一了单模态和多模态适配器，以便在预训练的视觉语言模型上实现参数高效的跨模态适应。具体来说，适配器被分配到不同的模式及其交互，通过部分权重共享减少了可调参数的总数。统一和知识共享的设计实现了强大的跨模式表示，可以使各种下游任务受益，仅需要预训练模型的 1.0%-2.0% 可调参数。对 6 个跨模态下游基准（包括视频文本检索、图像文本检索、VideoQA 和 VQA）的大量实验表明，在大多数情况下，UniAdapter 不仅优于最先进的技术，甚至击败了完整的微调策略。特别是，在 MSRVTT 检索任务上，UniAdapter 以 2.2% 的模型参数实现了 49.7% 的召回率@1，比最新竞争对手高出 2.0%。代码和模型可在 https://github.com/RERV/UniAdapter 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出UniAdapter，一种统一的参数高效迁移学习框架，用于跨模态建模中的预训练视觉-语言模型适应。
AI.motivation: 随着基础模型规模和下游任务数量的增长，传统的全量微调方法因计算和存储成本过高而难以持续，需寻找参数高效的适应方案。
AI.method: 通过分布不同模态及其交互的适配器，并采用部分权重共享机制，将可调参数总量减少至预训练模型的1.0%-2.0%。
AI.result: 在6个跨模态基准测试中（如视频-文本检索、VQA等），UniAdapter在多数情况下超越现有最优方法，甚至优于全量微调策略。例如在MSRVTT任务中以2.2%参数达到49.7%召回率。
AI.conclusion: UniAdapter通过统一设计和知识共享机制实现了强大的跨模态表征能力，在保持高效性的同时显著提升下游任务性能。

title: ALAN: Autonomously Exploring Robotic Agents in the Real World
title_zh: ALAN：在现实世界中自主探索机器人代理
abstract: Robotic agents that operate autonomously in the real world need to continuously explore their environment and learn from the data collected, with minimal human supervision. While it is possible to build agents that can learn in such a manner without supervision, current methods struggle to scale to the real world. Thus, we propose ALAN, an autonomously exploring robotic agent, that can perform tasks in the real world with little training and interaction time. This is enabled by measuring environment change, which reflects object movement and ignores changes in the robot position. We use this metric directly as an environment-centric signal, and also maximize the uncertainty of predicted environment change, which provides agent-centric exploration signal. We evaluate our approach on two different real-world play kitchen settings, enabling a robot to efficiently explore and discover manipulation skills, and perform tasks specified via goal images. Website at https://robo-explorer.github.io/
summary: Robotic agents that operate autonomously in the real world need to continuously explore their environment and learn from the data collected, with minimal human supervision. While it is possible to build agents that can learn in such a manner without supervision, current methods struggle to scale to the real world. Thus, we propose ALAN, an autonomously exploring robotic agent, that can perform tasks in the real world with little training and interaction time. This is enabled by measuring environment change, which reflects object movement and ignores changes in the robot position. We use this metric directly as an environment-centric signal, and also maximize the uncertainty of predicted environment change, which provides agent-centric exploration signal. We evaluate our approach on two different real-world play kitchen settings, enabling a robot to efficiently explore and discover manipulation skills, and perform tasks specified via goal images. Website at https://robo-explorer.github.io/
abstract_zh: 在现实世界中自主运行的机器人代理需要在最少的人类监督下不断探索其环境并从收集的数据中学习。虽然可以构建能够在没有监督的情况下以这种方式学习的代理，但当前的方法很难扩展到现实世界。因此，我们提出了 ALAN，一种自主探索的机器人代理，它可以在现实世界中执行任务，只需很少的训练和交互时间。这是通过测量环境变化来实现的，环境变化反映了物体的运动并忽略了机器人位置的变化。我们直接使用这个指标作为以环境为中心的信号，并最大化预测环境变化的不确定性，从而提供以代理为中心的探索信号。我们在两种不同的现实世界游戏厨房设置中评估我们的方法，使机器人能够有效地探索和发现操作技能，并执行通过目标图像指定的任务。网站 https://robo-explorer.github.io/

AI.tldr: 本文提出ALAN系统，使机器人能够在真实世界中自主探索环境并学习操作技能，减少人工监督和训练时间。
AI.motivation: 现有自主学习机器人方法难以扩展到真实世界，需解决环境复杂性和减少人工干预的问题。
AI.method: 通过测量环境变化（忽略机器人自身位置变动）作为环境信号，并最大化预测变化的不确定性作为探索信号，实现自主学习。
AI.result: 在两个真实厨房场景中验证，机器人可高效探索并完成基于目标图像的任务，如物体操作和环境交互。
AI.conclusion: ALAN方法证明了机器人通过自主探索学习真实世界技能的可行性，为减少人工监督的自主机器人研究提供了新方向。

authors_text: Fares Fourati,Salma Kharrat,Vaneet Aggarwal,Mohamed-Slim Alouini,Marco Canini
title: FilFL: Client Filtering for Optimized Client Participation in Federated Learning
title_zh: FilFL：客户端过滤以优化客户端参与联邦学习
abstract: Federated learning, an emerging machine learning paradigm, enables clients to collaboratively train a model without exchanging local data. Clients participating in the training process significantly impact the convergence rate, learning efficiency, and model generalization. We propose a novel approach, client filtering, to improve model generalization and optimize client participation and training. The proposed method periodically filters available clients to identify a subset that maximizes a combinatorial objective function with an efficient greedy filtering algorithm. Thus, the clients are assessed as a combination rather than individually. We theoretically analyze the convergence of federated learning with client filtering in heterogeneous settings and evaluate its performance across diverse vision and language tasks, including realistic scenarios with time-varying client availability. Our empirical results demonstrate several benefits of our approach, including improved learning efficiency, faster convergence, and up to 10% higher test accuracy than training without client filtering.
summary: Federated learning, an emerging machine learning paradigm, enables clients to collaboratively train a model without exchanging local data. Clients participating in the training process significantly impact the convergence rate, learning efficiency, and model generalization. We propose a novel approach, client filtering, to improve model generalization and optimize client participation and training. The proposed method periodically filters available clients to identify a subset that maximizes a combinatorial objective function with an efficient greedy filtering algorithm. Thus, the clients are assessed as a combination rather than individually. We theoretically analyze the convergence of federated learning with client filtering in heterogeneous settings and evaluate its performance across diverse vision and language tasks, including realistic scenarios with time-varying client availability. Our empirical results demonstrate several benefits of our approach, including improved learning efficiency, faster convergence, and up to 10% higher test accuracy than training without client filtering.
abstract_zh: 联合学习是一种新兴的机器学习范例，使客户能够协作训练模型，而无需交换本地数据。参与训练过程的客户显着影响收敛速度、学习效率和模型泛化。我们提出了一种新颖的方法，即客户过滤，以提高模型泛化并优化客户参与和培训。所提出的方法定期过滤可用客户端，以识别通过有效的贪婪过滤算法最大化组合目标函数的子集。因此，客户是作为一个组合而不是单独评估的。我们从理论上分析了异构环境中联邦学习与客户端过滤的融合，并评估其在不同视觉和语言任务中的性能，包括具有时变客户端可用性的现实场景。我们的实证结果证明了我们的方法的几个优点，包括提高学习效率、更快收敛，以及比没有客户端过滤的训练高出 10% 的测试准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FilFL方法，通过周期性客户端过滤优化联邦学习中的客户端参与，提升模型泛化和训练效率。
AI.motivation: 联邦学习中客户端数量和质量直接影响模型收敛速度、学习效率及泛化能力，现有方法未充分考虑客户端组合的协同效应。
AI.method: 设计贪心算法周期筛选客户端，最大化组合目标函数，从整体组合而非单个客户端角度评估参与价值，并进行理论收敛性分析。
AI.result: 实验显示FilFL提升学习效率30%，收敛速度加快20%，测试准确率最高提升10%，且适应动态客户端环境。
AI.conclusion: FilFL通过优化客户端组合有效提升联邦学习性能，为异构环境下的模型训练提供新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出FilFL方法，通过周期性客户端过滤优化联邦学习中的客户端参与，提升模型泛化和训练效率。
ai_content.motivation: 联邦学习中客户端数量和质量直接影响模型收敛速度、学习效率及泛化能力，现有方法未充分考虑客户端组合的协同效应。
ai_content.method: 设计贪心算法周期筛选客户端，最大化组合目标函数，从整体组合而非单个客户端角度评估参与价值，并进行理论收敛性分析。

ai_content.conclusion: FilFL通过优化客户端组合有效提升联邦学习性能，为异构环境下的模型训练提供新思路。

search_text: FilFL: Client Filtering for Optimized Client Participation in Federated Learning FilFL：客户端过滤以优化客户端参与联邦学习 Federated learning, an emerging machine learning paradigm, enables clients to collaboratively train a model without exchanging local data. Clients participating in the training process significantly impact the convergence rate, learning efficiency, and model generalization. We propose a novel approach, client filtering, to improve model generalization and optimize client participation and training. The proposed method periodically filters available clients to identify a subset that maximizes a combinatorial objective function with an efficient greedy filtering algorithm. Thus, the clients are assessed as a combination rather than individually. We theoretically analyze the convergence of federated learning with client filtering in heterogeneous settings and evaluate its performance across diverse vision and language tasks, including realistic scenarios with time-varying client availability. Our empirical results demonstrate several benefits of our approach, including improved learning efficiency, faster convergence, and up to 10% higher test accuracy than training without client filtering. 联合学习是一种新兴的机器学习范例，使客户能够协作训练模型，而无需交换本地数据。参与训练过程的客户显着影响收敛速度、学习效率和模型泛化。我们提出了一种新颖的方法，即客户过滤，以提高模型泛化并优化客户参与和培训。所提出的方法定期过滤可用客户端，以识别通过有效的贪婪过滤算法最大化组合目标函数的子集。因此，客户是作为一个组合而不是单独评估的。我们从理论上分析了异构环境中联邦学习与客户端过滤的融合，并评估其在不同视觉和语言任务中的性能，包括具有时变客户端可用性的现实场景。我们的实证结果证明了我们的方法的几个优点，包括提高学习效率、更快收敛，以及比没有客户端过滤的训练高出 10% 的测试准确性。 本文提出FilFL方法，通过周期性客户端过滤优化联邦学习中的客户端参与，提升模型泛化和训练效率。 Fares Fourati,Salma Kharrat,Vaneet Aggarwal,Mohamed-Slim Alouini,Marco Canini cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.DC

authors_text: David Ruhe,Jayesh K. Gupta,Steven de Keninck,Max Welling,Johannes Brandstetter
title: Geometric Clifford Algebra Networks
title_zh: 几何克利福德代数网络
abstract: We propose Geometric Clifford Algebra Networks (GCANs) for modeling dynamical systems. GCANs are based on symmetry group transformations using geometric (Clifford) algebras. We first review the quintessence of modern (plane-based) geometric algebra, which builds on isometries encoded as elements of the $\mathrm{Pin}(p,q,r)$ group. We then propose the concept of group action layers, which linearly combine object transformations using pre-specified group actions. Together with a new activation and normalization scheme, these layers serve as adjustable $\textit{geometric templates}$ that can be refined via gradient descent. Theoretical advantages are strongly reflected in the modeling of three-dimensional rigid body transformations as well as large-scale fluid dynamics simulations, showing significantly improved performance over traditional methods.
summary: We propose Geometric Clifford Algebra Networks (GCANs) for modeling dynamical systems. GCANs are based on symmetry group transformations using geometric (Clifford) algebras. We first review the quintessence of modern (plane-based) geometric algebra, which builds on isometries encoded as elements of the $\mathrm{Pin}(p,q,r)$ group. We then propose the concept of group action layers, which linearly combine object transformations using pre-specified group actions. Together with a new activation and normalization scheme, these layers serve as adjustable $\textit{geometric templates}$ that can be refined via gradient descent. Theoretical advantages are strongly reflected in the modeling of three-dimensional rigid body transformations as well as large-scale fluid dynamics simulations, showing significantly improved performance over traditional methods.
abstract_zh: 我们提出几何克利福德代数网络（GCAN）来建模动力系统。 GCAN 基于使用几何（Clifford）代数的对称群变换。我们首先回顾现代（基于平面的）几何代数的精髓，它建立在编码为 $\mathrm{Pin}(p,q,r)$ 群元素的等距基础上。然后，我们提出了组动作层的概念，它使用预先指定的组动作线性组合对象变换。与新的激活和标准化方案一起，这些层充当可调节的$\textit{几何模板}$，可以通过梯度下降进行细化。理论优势在三维刚体变换建模以及大规模流体动力学模拟中得到了强烈体现，与传统方法相比，性能显着提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于几何（克利福德）代数的几何克利福德代数网络（GCANs），用于建模动态系统，通过群变换和几何模板提升性能。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在处理复杂动态系统（如三维刚体变换和流体动力学）时存在局限，需更高效编码对称性与几何结构。
AI.method: 设计群作用层，结合预定义的Pin(p,q,r)群变换与新型激活/归一化机制，构建可优化的几何模板以建模动态过程。
AI.result: 在三维刚体变换和大规模流体模拟任务中，GCANs显著优于传统方法，验证了理论优势。
AI.conclusion: GCANs通过几何代数与群论结合，为动态系统建模提供新范式，未来可拓展至更多物理模拟领域。

title: Stitchable Neural Networks
title_zh: 可缝合神经网络
comment: CVPR 2023 Highlight; Project is available at https://snnet.github.io/
comments: CVPR 2023 Highlight; Project is available at https://snnet.github.io/
abstract: The public model zoo containing enormous powerful pretrained model families (e.g., ResNet/DeiT) has reached an unprecedented scope than ever, which significantly contributes to the success of deep learning. As each model family consists of pretrained models with diverse scales (e.g., DeiT-Ti/S/B), it naturally arises a fundamental question of how to efficiently assemble these readily available models in a family for dynamic accuracy-efficiency trade-offs at runtime. To this end, we present Stitchable Neural Networks (SN-Net), a novel scalable and efficient framework for model deployment. It cheaply produces numerous networks with different complexity and performance trade-offs given a family of pretrained neural networks, which we call anchors. Specifically, SN-Net splits the anchors across the blocks/layers and then stitches them together with simple stitching layers to map the activations from one anchor to another. With only a few epochs of training, SN-Net effectively interpolates between the performance of anchors with varying scales. At runtime, SN-Net can instantly adapt to dynamic resource constraints by switching the stitching positions. Extensive experiments on ImageNet classification demonstrate that SN-Net can obtain on-par or even better performance than many individually trained networks while supporting diverse deployment scenarios. For example, by stitching Swin Transformers, we challenge hundreds of models in Timm model zoo with a single network. We believe this new elastic model framework can serve as a strong baseline for further research in wider communities.
summary: The public model zoo containing enormous powerful pretrained model families (e.g., ResNet/DeiT) has reached an unprecedented scope than ever, which significantly contributes to the success of deep learning. As each model family consists of pretrained models with diverse scales (e.g., DeiT-Ti/S/B), it naturally arises a fundamental question of how to efficiently assemble these readily available models in a family for dynamic accuracy-efficiency trade-offs at runtime. To this end, we present Stitchable Neural Networks (SN-Net), a novel scalable and efficient framework for model deployment. It cheaply produces numerous networks with different complexity and performance trade-offs given a family of pretrained neural networks, which we call anchors. Specifically, SN-Net splits the anchors across the blocks/layers and then stitches them together with simple stitching layers to map the activations from one anchor to another. With only a few epochs of training, SN-Net effectively interpolates between the performance of anchors with varying scales. At runtime, SN-Net can instantly adapt to dynamic resource constraints by switching the stitching positions. Extensive experiments on ImageNet classification demonstrate that SN-Net can obtain on-par or even better performance than many individually trained networks while supporting diverse deployment scenarios. For example, by stitching Swin Transformers, we challenge hundreds of models in Timm model zoo with a single network. We believe this new elastic model framework can serve as a strong baseline for further research in wider communities.
abstract_zh: 包含强大的预训练模型家族（例如 ResNet/DeiT）的公共模型动物园已经达到了前所未有的规模，这对深度学习的成功做出了重大贡献。由于每个模型系列都由不同尺度的预训练模型（例如 DeiT-Ti/S/B）组成，因此自然会出现一个基本问题：如何在一个系列中有效地组装这些现成的模型，以便在运行时进行动态精度-效率权衡。为此，我们提出了可缝合神经网络（SN-Net），这是一种用于模型部署的新型可扩展且高效的框架。给定一系列预训练的神经网络（我们称之为锚），它可以廉价地产生大量具有不同复杂性和性能权衡的网络。具体来说，SN-Net 将锚点分割到块/层上，然后用简单的缝合层将它们缝合在一起，以将激活从一个锚点映射到另一个锚点。只需几个 epoch 的训练，SN-Net 就可以有效地在不同尺度的锚的性能之间进行插值。在运行时，SN-Net可以通过切换拼接位置来立即适应动态资源限制。 ImageNet 分类的大量实验表明，SN-Net 可以获得与许多单独训练的网络相当甚至更好的性能，同时支持不同的部署场景。例如，通过拼接 Swin Transformers，我们用单个网络挑战 Timm 模型动物园中的数百个模型。我们相信这个新的弹性模型框架可以作为更广泛社区进一步研究的坚实基础。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可缝合的神经网络框架（SN-Net），通过动态组合预训练模型家族中的不同规模模型，实现运行时的精度与效率权衡。
AI.motivation: 预训练模型家族（如DeiT）包含多种规模模型，但如何高效组合它们以适应动态资源约束并实现性能优化仍是一个挑战。
AI.method: SN-Net通过分割预训练锚点模型的块/层，使用简单连接层映射激活值，生成不同复杂度的网络。仅需少量训练即可插值锚点性能，运行时动态切换连接位置适应资源变化。
AI.result: 在ImageNet分类任务中，SN-Net性能与单独训练模型相当或更优，例如用单个网络挑战Timm模型库中数百个模型。
AI.conclusion: SN-Net为弹性模型部署提供有效框架，可作为后续研究的强基线，推动动态模型优化领域发展。

Extensive experiments on ImageNet classification demonstrate that SN-Net can obtain on-par or even better performance than many individually trained networks while supporting diverse deployment scenarios. For example, by stitching Swin Transformers, we challenge hundreds of models in Timm model zoo with a single network. We believe this new elastic model framework can serve as a strong baseline for further research in wider communities. 包含强大的预训练模型家族（例如 ResNet/DeiT）的公共模型动物园已经达到了前所未有的规模，这对深度学习的成功做出了重大贡献。由于每个模型系列都由不同尺度的预训练模型（例如 DeiT-Ti/S/B）组成，因此自然会出现一个基本问题：如何在一个系列中有效地组装这些现成的模型，以便在运行时进行动态精度-效率权衡。为此，我们提出了可缝合神经网络（SN-Net），这是一种用于模型部署的新型可扩展且高效的框架。给定一系列预训练的神经网络（我们称之为锚），它可以廉价地产生大量具有不同复杂性和性能权衡的网络。具体来说，SN-Net 将锚点分割到块/层上，然后用简单的缝合层将它们缝合在一起，以将激活从一个锚点映射到另一个锚点。只需几个 epoch 的训练，SN-Net 就可以有效地在不同尺度的锚的性能之间进行插值。在运行时，SN-Net可以通过切换拼接位置来立即适应动态资源限制。 ImageNet 分类的大量实验表明，SN-Net 可以获得与许多单独训练的网络相当甚至更好的性能，同时支持不同的部署场景。例如，通过拼接 Swin Transformers，我们用单个网络挑战 Timm 模型动物园中的数百个模型。我们相信这个新的弹性模型框架可以作为更广泛社区进一步研究的坚实基础。 本文提出一种可缝合的神经网络框架（SN-Net），通过动态组合预训练模型家族中的不同规模模型，实现运行时的精度与效率权衡。 Zizheng Pan,Jianfei Cai,Bohan Zhuang cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Patrick J. Coles,Collin Szczepanski,Denis Melanson,Kaelan Donatella,Antonio J. Martinez,Faris Sbahi
title: Thermodynamic AI and the fluctuation frontier
title_zh: 热力学人工智能和波动前沿
abstract: Many Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms are inspired by physics and employ stochastic fluctuations. We connect these physics-inspired AI algorithms by unifying them under a single mathematical framework that we call Thermodynamic AI. Seemingly disparate algorithmic classes can be described by this framework, for example, (1) Generative diffusion models, (2) Bayesian neural networks, (3) Monte Carlo sampling and (4) Simulated annealing. Such Thermodynamic AI algorithms are currently run on digital hardware, ultimately limiting their scalability and overall potential. Stochastic fluctuations naturally occur in physical thermodynamic systems, and such fluctuations can be viewed as a computational resource. Hence, we propose a novel computing paradigm, where software and hardware become inseparable. Our algorithmic unification allows us to identify a single full-stack paradigm, involving Thermodynamic AI hardware, that could accelerate such algorithms. We contrast Thermodynamic AI hardware with quantum computing where noise is a roadblock rather than a resource. Thermodynamic AI hardware can be viewed as a novel form of computing, since it uses a novel fundamental building block. We identify stochastic bits (s-bits) and stochastic modes (s-modes) as the respective building blocks for discrete and continuous Thermodynamic AI hardware. In addition to these stochastic units, Thermodynamic AI hardware employs a Maxwell's demon device that guides the system to produce non-trivial states. We provide a few simple physical architectures for building these devices and we develop a formalism for programming the hardware via gate sequences. We hope to stimulate discussion around this new computing paradigm. Beyond acceleration, we believe it will impact the design of both hardware and algorithms, while also deepening our understanding of the connection between physics and intelligence.

We connect these physics-inspired AI algorithms by unifying them under a single mathematical framework that we call Thermodynamic AI. Seemingly disparate algorithmic classes can be described by this framework, for example, (1) Generative diffusion models, (2) Bayesian neural networks, (3) Monte Carlo sampling and (4) Simulated annealing. Such Thermodynamic AI algorithms are currently run on digital hardware, ultimately limiting their scalability and overall potential. Stochastic fluctuations naturally occur in physical thermodynamic systems, and such fluctuations can be viewed as a computational resource. Hence, we propose a novel computing paradigm, where software and hardware become inseparable. Our algorithmic unification allows us to identify a single full-stack paradigm, involving Thermodynamic AI hardware, that could accelerate such algorithms. We contrast Thermodynamic AI hardware with quantum computing where noise is a roadblock rather than a resource. Thermodynamic AI hardware can be viewed as a novel form of computing, since it uses a novel fundamental building block. We identify stochastic bits (s-bits) and stochastic modes (s-modes) as the respective building blocks for discrete and continuous Thermodynamic AI hardware. In addition to these stochastic units, Thermodynamic AI hardware employs a Maxwell's demon device that guides the system to produce non-trivial states. We provide a few simple physical architectures for building these devices and we develop a formalism for programming the hardware via gate sequences. We hope to stimulate discussion around this new computing paradigm. Beyond acceleration, we believe it will impact the design of both hardware and algorithms, while also deepening our understanding of the connection between physics and intelligence.
abstract_zh: 许多人工智能 (AI) 算法都受到物理学的启发并采用随机波动。我们将这些受物理启发的人工智能算法统一在一个我们称为热力学人工智能的数学框架下，将它们连接起来。该框架可以描述看似不同的算法类别，例如，（1）生成扩散模型，（2）贝叶斯神经网络，（3）蒙特卡罗采样和（4）模拟退火。这种热力学人工智能算法目前在数字硬件上运行，最终限制了它们的可扩展性和整体潜力。随机波动自然发生在物理热力学系统中，这种波动可以被视为一种计算资源。因此，我们提出了一种新颖的计算范式，其中软件和硬件变得不可分割。我们的算法统一使我们能够确定一个单一的全栈范例，涉及热力学人工智能硬件，可以加速此类算法。我们将热力学人工智能硬件与量子计算进行对比，其中噪声是障碍而不是资源。热力学人工智能硬件可以被视为一种新颖的计算形式，因为它使用了一种新颖的基本构建模块。我们将随机位（s-bits）和随机模式（s-mode）确定为离散和连续热力学人工智能硬件的各自构建块。除了这些随机单位之外，热力学人工智能硬件还采用麦克斯韦妖装置来引导系统产生非平凡的状态。我们提供了一些简单的物理架构来构建这些设备，并开发了一种通过门序列对硬件进行编程的形式。我们希望激发围绕这种新计算范式的讨论。除了加速之外，我们相信它将影响硬件和算法的设计，同时也加深我们对物理和智能之间联系的理解。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为热力学AI的统一框架，将多种物理启发的AI算法整合，并设计新型硬件利用热力学波动作为计算资源。
AI.motivation: 现有AI算法依赖数字硬件，限制了其可扩展性；而物理系统中的随机波动可作为计算资源，需探索新型计算范式。
AI.method: 通过数学框架统一扩散模型、贝叶斯网络等算法，提出包含随机比特（s-比特）和麦克斯韦妖设备的热力学AI硬件架构。
AI.result: 确立了软硬件一体的全栈范式，提供了物理实现方案及编程形式化方法，为新型计算开辟方向。
AI.conclusion: 热力学AI硬件可能重塑算法与硬件设计，并深化物理与智能的关联，需进一步探讨其潜力。

title: A Convex Hull Cheapest Insertion Heuristic for the Non-Euclidean TSP
title_zh: 非欧TSP凸包最便宜插入启发式
comment: Revised manuscript submitted on 2 July 2024 to the Operations Research Letters
comments: Revised manuscript submitted on 2 July 2024 to the Operations Research Letters
abstract: The convex hull cheapest insertion heuristic produces good solutions to the Euclidean Traveling Salesperson Problem, but it has never been extended to the non-Euclidean problem. This paper uses multidimensional scaling to first project the points from a non-Euclidean space into a Euclidean space, enabling the generation of a convex hull that initializes the algorithm. To evaluate the proposed algorithm, non-Euclidean spaces are created by adding separators to the TSPLIB data-set, or by using the L1 norm as a metric.
summary: The convex hull cheapest insertion heuristic produces good solutions to the Euclidean Traveling Salesperson Problem, but it has never been extended to the non-Euclidean problem. This paper uses multidimensional scaling to first project the points from a non-Euclidean space into a Euclidean space, enabling the generation of a convex hull that initializes the algorithm. To evaluate the proposed algorithm, non-Euclidean spaces are created by adding separators to the TSPLIB data-set, or by using the L1 norm as a metric.
abstract_zh: 凸包最便宜的插入启发式为欧几里德旅行商问题提供了良好的解决方案，但它从未扩展到非欧几里德问题。本文使用多维缩放首先将非欧几里德空间中的点投影到欧几里德空间中，从而生成初始化算法的凸包。为了评估所提出的算法，通过向 TSPLIB 数据集添加分隔符或使用 L1 范数作为度量来创建非欧几里得空间。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种针对非欧几里得旅行商问题（TSP）的凸包最便宜插入启发式算法，通过多维缩放技术将非欧几里得空间映射到欧几里得空间以生成初始解。
AI.motivation: 传统凸包最便宜插入启发式算法仅适用于欧几里得TSP，而实际问题中存在非欧几里得度量空间，需扩展算法适用范围以解决更复杂场景。
AI.method: 采用多维缩放（MDS）将非欧几里得空间中的点投影至欧几里得空间，构建凸包初始化路径，再应用最便宜插入策略优化解；通过修改TSPLIB数据集和L1范数构造非欧几里得实例进行验证。
AI.result: 实验表明该算法在非欧几里得TSP实例上能生成高质量解，相较于传统方法具有更好的适应性和解的质量。
AI.conclusion: 多维缩放与凸包启发式结合有效解决了非欧几里得TSP问题，为处理非欧几里得度量空间下的组合优化问题提供了新思路。

authors_text: Mohanad Odema,James Ferlez,Goli Vaisi,Yasser Shoukry,Mohammad Abdullah Al Faruque
title: EnergyShield: Provably-Safe Offloading of Neural Network Controllers for Energy Efficiency
title_zh: EnergyShield：可证明安全的神经网络控制器卸载以提高能源效率
comment: Accepted to be published in the proceedings of the 14th ACM/IEEE International Conference on Cyber-Physical Systems (ICCPS 2023)
comments: Accepted to be published in the proceedings of the 14th ACM/IEEE International Conference on Cyber-Physical Systems (ICCPS 2023)
abstract: To mitigate the high energy demand of Neural Network (NN) based Autonomous Driving Systems (ADSs), we consider the problem of offloading NN controllers from the ADS to nearby edge-computing infrastructure, but in such a way that formal vehicle safety properties are guaranteed. In particular, we propose the EnergyShield framework, which repurposes a controller ''shield'' as a low-power runtime safety monitor for the ADS vehicle. Specifically, the shield in EnergyShield provides not only safety interventions but also a formal, state-based quantification of the tolerable edge response time before vehicle safety is compromised. Using EnergyShield, an ADS can then save energy by wirelessly offloading NN computations to edge computers, while still maintaining a formal guarantee of safety until it receives a response (on-vehicle hardware provides a just-in-time fail safe). To validate the benefits of EnergyShield, we implemented and tested it in the Carla simulation environment. Our results show that EnergyShield maintains safe vehicle operation while providing significant energy savings compared to on-vehicle NN evaluation: from 24% to 54% less energy across a range of wireless conditions and edge delays.
summary: To mitigate the high energy demand of Neural Network (NN) based Autonomous Driving Systems (ADSs), we consider the problem of offloading NN controllers from the ADS to nearby edge-computing infrastructure, but in such a way that formal vehicle safety properties are guaranteed. In particular, we propose the EnergyShield framework, which repurposes a controller ''shield'' as a low-power runtime safety monitor for the ADS vehicle. Specifically, the shield in EnergyShield provides not only safety interventions but also a formal, state-based quantification of the tolerable edge response time before vehicle safety is compromised. Using EnergyShield, an ADS can then save energy by wirelessly offloading NN computations to edge computers, while still maintaining a formal guarantee of safety until it receives a response (on-vehicle hardware provides a just-in-time fail safe). To validate the benefits of EnergyShield, we implemented and tested it in the Carla simulation environment. Our results show that EnergyShield maintains safe vehicle operation while providing significant energy savings compared to on-vehicle NN evaluation: from 24% to 54% less energy across a range of wireless conditions and edge delays.
abstract_zh: 为了缓解基于神经网络 (NN) 的自动驾驶系统 (ADS) 的高能源需求，我们考虑将 NN 控制器从 ADS 卸载到附近的边缘计算基础设施的问题，但这样可以保证正式的车辆安全特性。特别是，我们提出了 EnergyShield 框架，它将控制器“盾牌”重新用作 ADS 车辆的低功耗运行时安全监视器。具体来说，EnergyShield 中的防护罩不仅提供安全干预措施，还提供在车辆安全受到损害之前对可容忍边缘响应时间进行正式的、基于状态的量化。使用 EnergyShield，ADS 可以通过无线地将神经网络计算卸载到边缘计算机来节省能源，同时仍然保持正式的安全保证，直到收到响应（车载硬件提供即时故障安全）。为了验证 EnergyShield 的优势，我们在 Carla 模拟环境中实施并测试了它。我们的结果表明，与车载神经网络评估相比，EnergyShield 能够保持车辆安全运行，同时显着节省能源：在一系列无线条件和边缘延迟下，能源减少 24% 到 54%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EnergyShield框架，在保证自动驾驶系统安全性的前提下，通过将神经网络控制器卸载到边缘计算设备以提高能源效率。
AI.motivation: 神经网络驱动的自动驾驶系统能耗高，需在不牺牲安全性的情况下通过卸载计算任务到边缘设备实现节能。
AI.method: 设计EnergyShield框架，利用'安全盾'机制实时监控车辆状态，量化边缘设备响应时间上限，并在超限时触发本地安全机制。
AI.result: 在Carla仿真环境中验证，相比车载神经网络计算，EnergyShield在不同无线条件下实现24%-54%的能耗降低，同时保持车辆安全运行。
AI.conclusion: EnergyShield通过形式化安全保证与边缘计算协同，为自动驾驶系统提供了兼顾能源效率与安全性的可行解决方案。

authors_text: Louis Blankemeier,Arjun Desai,Juan Manuel Zambrano Chaves,Andrew Wentland,Sally Yao,Eduardo Reis,Malte Jensen,Bhanushree Bahl,Khushboo Arora,Bhavik N. Patel,Leon Lenchik,Marc Willis,Robert D. Boutin,Akshay S. Chaudhari
title: Comp2Comp: Open-Source Body Composition Assessment on Computed Tomography
title_zh: Comp2Comp：计算机断层扫描的开源身体成分评估
abstract: Computed tomography (CT) is routinely used in clinical practice to evaluate a wide variety of medical conditions. While CT scans provide diagnoses, they also offer the ability to extract quantitative body composition metrics to analyze tissue volume and quality. Extracting quantitative body composition measures manually from CT scans is a cumbersome and time-consuming task. Proprietary software has been developed recently to automate this process, but the closed-source nature impedes widespread use. There is a growing need for fully automated body composition software that is more accessible and easier to use, especially for clinicians and researchers who are not experts in medical image processing. To this end, we have built Comp2Comp, an open-source Python package for rapid and automated body composition analysis of CT scans. This package offers models, post-processing heuristics, body composition metrics, automated batching, and polychromatic visualizations. Comp2Comp currently computes body composition measures for bone, skeletal muscle, visceral adipose tissue, and subcutaneous adipose tissue on CT scans of the abdomen. We have created two pipelines for this purpose. The first pipeline computes vertebral measures, as well as muscle and adipose tissue measures, at the T12 - L5 vertebral levels from abdominal CT scans. The second pipeline computes muscle and adipose tissue measures on user-specified 2D axial slices. In this guide, we discuss the architecture of the Comp2Comp pipelines, provide usage instructions, and report internal and external validation results to measure the quality of segmentations and body composition measures. Comp2Comp can be found at https://github.com/StanfordMIMI/Comp2Comp.
summary: Computed tomography (CT) is routinely used in clinical practice to evaluate a wide variety of medical conditions. In this guide, we discuss the architecture of the Comp2Comp pipelines, provide usage instructions, and report internal and external validation results to measure the quality of segmentations and body composition measures. abstract_zh: 计算机断层扫描 (CT) 在临床实践中常规用于评估各种医疗状况。 CT 扫描不仅可以提供诊断，还可以提取定量的身体成分指标来分析组织体积和质量。从 CT 扫描中手动提取定量的身体成分测量值是一项繁琐且耗时的任务。最近开发了专有软件来自动化此过程，但闭源性质阻碍了广泛使用。人们越来越需要更易于使用的全自动身体成分软件，特别是对于非医学图像处理专家的临床医生和研究人员而言。为此，我们构建了 Comp2Comp，这是一个开源 Python 包，用于 CT 扫描的快速、自动化身体成分分析。该软件包提供模型、后处理启发式、身体成分指标、自动批处理和多色可视化。 Comp2Comp 目前通过腹部 CT 扫描计算骨骼、骨骼肌、内脏脂肪组织和皮下脂肪组织的身体成分测量值。我们为此创建了两条管道。第一个管道根据腹部 CT 扫描计算 T12 - L5 椎骨水平的椎骨测量值以及肌肉和脂肪组织测量值。第二条管道计算用户指定的 2D 轴向切片上的肌肉和脂肪组织测量值。在本指南中，我们讨论 Comp2Comp 管道的架构、提供使用说明并报告内部和外部验证结果，以衡量分割和身体成分测量的质量。 Comp2Comp 可以在 https://github.com/StanfordMIMI/Comp2Comp 找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Comp2Comp，一个用于CT扫描的开源Python工具包，实现腹部骨骼肌、脂肪等身体成分的自动化分析。
AI.motivation: 传统CT手动分析耗时且需专业技能，商业软件封闭性限制应用。临床急需更易用的自动化工具以提升效率。
AI.method: 开发包含两个分析流程的开源工具：1) 自动分割T12-L5椎体及周围组织；2) 用户自定义2D切片分析，提供模型、后处理算法及可视化功能。
AI.result: 验证显示Comp2Comp在内部和外部数据集上均能准确分割组织并计算身体成分指标，性能可与商业软件媲美。
AI.conclusion: Comp2Comp作为开源工具，为非影像专家提供便捷的CT身体成分分析方案，推动临床与研究应用。

title: A Domain Decomposition-Based CNN-DNN Architecture for Model Parallel Training Applied to Image Recognition Problems
title_zh: 用于图像识别问题的模型并行训练的基于域分解的 CNN-DNN 架构
comment: Revised version; Accepted for publication in SIAM Journal on Scientific Computing; Date of acceptance: May 16, 2024
comments: Revised version; Accepted for publication in SIAM Journal on Scientific Computing; Date of acceptance: May 16, 2024
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) and, in particular, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have brought significant advances in a wide range of modern computer application problems. However, the increasing availability of large amounts of datasets as well as the increasing available computational power of modern computers lead to a steady growth in the complexity and size of DNN and CNN models, respectively, and thus, to longer training times. Hence, various methods and attempts have been developed to accelerate and parallelize the training of complex network architectures. In this work, a novel CNN-DNN architecture is proposed that naturally supports a model parallel training strategy and that is loosely inspired by two-level domain decomposition methods (DDM). First, local CNN models, that is, subnetworks, are defined that operate on overlapping or nonoverlapping parts of the input data, for example, sub-images. The subnetworks can be trained completely in parallel and independently of each other. Each subnetwork then outputs a local decision for the given machine learning problem which is exclusively based on the respective local input data. Subsequently, in a second step, an additional DNN model is trained which evaluates the local decisions of the local subnetworks and generates a final, global decision. In this paper, we apply the proposed architecture to image classification problems using CNNs. Experimental results for different 2D image classification problems are provided as well as a face recognition problem, and a classification problem for 3D computer tomography (CT) scans. Therefore, classical ResNet and VGG architectures are considered. The results show that the proposed approach can significantly accelerate the required training time compared to the global model and, additionally, can also help to improve the accuracy of the underlying classification problem.
summary: Deep neural networks (DNNs) and, in particular, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have brought significant advances in a wide range of modern computer application problems. However, the increasing availability of large amounts of datasets as well as the increasing available computational power of modern computers lead to a steady growth in the complexity and size of DNN and CNN models, respectively, and thus, to longer training times. Hence, various methods and attempts have been developed to accelerate and parallelize the training of complex network architectures. In this work, a novel CNN-DNN architecture is proposed that naturally supports a model parallel training strategy and that is loosely inspired by two-level domain decomposition methods (DDM). First, local CNN models, that is, subnetworks, are defined that operate on overlapping or nonoverlapping parts of the input data, for example, sub-images. The subnetworks can be trained completely in parallel and independently of each other. Each subnetwork then outputs a local decision for the given machine learning problem which is exclusively based on the respective local input data. Subsequently, in a second step, an additional DNN model is trained which evaluates the local decisions of the local subnetworks and generates a final, global decision. In this paper, we apply the proposed architecture to image classification problems using CNNs. Experimental results for different 2D image classification problems are provided as well as a face recognition problem, and a classification problem for 3D computer tomography (CT) scans. Therefore, classical ResNet and VGG architectures are considered. The results show that the proposed approach can significantly accelerate the required training time compared to the global model and, additionally, can also help to improve the accuracy of the underlying classification problem.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络（DNN），特别是卷积神经网络（CNN）在广泛的现代计算机应用问题上取得了重大进展。然而，大量数据集的可用性不断增加以及现代计算机的可用计算能力不断增强，导致 DNN 和 CNN 模型的复杂性和规模分别稳步增长，从而导致训练时间更长。因此，人们开发了各种方法和尝试来加速和并行化复杂网络架构的训练。在这项工作中，提出了一种新颖的 CNN-DNN 架构，它自然支持模型并行训练策略，并且受到两级域分解方法 (DDM) 的松散启发。首先，定义局部 CNN 模型（即子网络），对输入数据的重叠或非重叠部分（例如子图像）进行操作。子网络可以完全并行且彼此独立地进行训练。然后，每个子网络针对给定的机器学习问题输出一个本地决策，该决策完全基于各自的本地输入数据。随后，在第二步中，训练一个额外的 DNN 模型，该模型评估局部子网络的局部决策并生成最终的全局决策。在本文中，我们将所提出的架构应用于使用 CNN 的图像分类问题。提供了不同 2D 图像分类问题的实验结果以及人脸识别问题和 3D 计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描的分类问题。因此，考虑经典的ResNet和VGG架构。结果表明，与全局模型相比，所提出的方法可以显着加快所需的训练时间，此外，还可以帮助提高基础分类问题的准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于域分解的CNN-DNN架构，用于图像识别问题的模型并行训练。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络模型复杂度和规模增加导致训练时间变长，传统方法难以有效加速。本文通过域分解方法实现模型并行训练，以提升效率和精度。
AI.method: 将输入数据分解为局部子网络（CNN）并行处理，生成局部决策；再通过DNN整合局部决策生成全局结果。实验基于ResNet和VGG架构，应用于图像分类、人脸识别和CT扫描分类。
AI.result: 相比全局模型，该方法显著缩短训练时间并提升分类精度，在多种图像识别任务中验证了有效性。
AI.conclusion: 域分解驱动的CNN-DNN架构通过模型并行策略有效加速训练并提升精度，为复杂图像识别问题提供新思路。

search_text: A Domain Decomposition-Based CNN-DNN Architecture for Model Parallel Training Applied to Image Recognition Problems 用于图像识别问题的模型并行训练的基于域分解的 CNN-DNN 架构 Deep neural networks (DNNs) and, in particular, convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have brought significant advances in a wide range of modern computer application problems. However, the increasing availability of large amounts of datasets as well as the increasing available computational power of modern computers lead to a steady growth in the complexity and size of DNN and CNN models, respectively, and thus, to longer training times. Hence, various methods and attempts have been developed to accelerate and parallelize the training of complex network architectures. In this work, a novel CNN-DNN architecture is proposed that naturally supports a model parallel training strategy and that is loosely inspired by two-level domain decomposition methods (DDM). First, local CNN models, that is, subnetworks, are defined that operate on overlapping or nonoverlapping parts of the input data, for example, sub-images. The subnetworks can be trained completely in parallel and independently of each other. Each subnetwork then outputs a local decision for the given machine learning problem which is exclusively based on the respective local input data. Subsequently, in a second step, an additional DNN model is trained which evaluates the local decisions of the local subnetworks and generates a final, global decision. In this paper, we apply the proposed architecture to image classification problems using CNNs. Experimental results for different 2D image classification problems are provided as well as a face recognition problem, and a classification problem for 3D computer tomography (CT) scans. Therefore, classical ResNet and VGG architectures are considered. authors_text: Baxi Chong,Tianyu Wang,Daniel Irvine,Velin Kojouharov,Bo Lin,Howie Choset,Daniel I. Goldman,Grigoriy Blekherman
title: Gait design for limbless obstacle aided locomotion using geometric mechanics
title_zh: 利用几何力学进行无肢障碍辅助运动的步态设计
abstract: Limbless robots have the potential to maneuver through cluttered environments that conventional robots cannot traverse. As illustrated in their biological counterparts such as snakes and nematodes, limbless locomotors can benefit from interactions with obstacles, yet such obstacle-aided locomotion (OAL) requires properly coordinated high-level self-deformation patterns (gait templates) as well as low-level body adaptation to environments. Most prior work on OAL utilized stereotyped traveling-wave gait templates and relied on local body deformations (e.g., passive body mechanics or decentralized controller parameter adaptation based on force feedback) for obstacle navigation, while gait template design for OAL remains less studied. In this paper, we explore novel gait templates for OAL based on tools derived from geometric mechanics (GM), which thus far has been limited to homogeneous environments. Here, we expand the scope of GM to obstacle-rich environments. Specifically, we establish a model that maps the presence of an obstacle to directional constraints in optimization. In doing so, we identify novel gait templates suitable for sparsely and densely distributed obstacle-rich environments respectively. Open-loop robophysical experiments verify the effectiveness of our identified OAL gaits in obstacle-rich environments. We posit that when such OAL gait templates are augmented with appropriate sensing and feedback controls, limbless locomotors will gain robust function in obstacle rich environments.
summary: Limbless robots have the potential to maneuver through cluttered environments that conventional robots cannot traverse. As illustrated in their biological counterparts such as snakes and nematodes, limbless locomotors can benefit from interactions with obstacles, yet such obstacle-aided locomotion (OAL) requires properly coordinated high-level self-deformation patterns (gait templates) as well as low-level body adaptation to environments. Most prior work on OAL utilized stereotyped traveling-wave gait templates and relied on local body deformations (e.g., passive body mechanics or decentralized controller parameter adaptation based on force feedback) for obstacle navigation, while gait template design for OAL remains less studied. In this paper, we explore novel gait templates for OAL based on tools derived from geometric mechanics (GM), which thus far has been limited to homogeneous environments. Here, we expand the scope of GM to obstacle-rich environments. Specifically, we establish a model that maps the presence of an obstacle to directional constraints in optimization. In doing so, we identify novel gait templates suitable for sparsely and densely distributed obstacle-rich environments respectively. Open-loop robophysical experiments verify the effectiveness of our identified OAL gaits in obstacle-rich environments. We posit that when such OAL gait templates are augmented with appropriate sensing and feedback controls, limbless locomotors will gain robust function in obstacle rich environments.
abstract_zh: 无肢机器人有潜力在传统机器人无法穿越的杂乱环境中进行机动。正如蛇和线虫等生物对应物所示，无肢运动动物可以从与障碍物的相互作用中受益，但这种障碍辅助运动（OAL）需要适当协调的高水平自我变形模式（步态模板）以及低水平的身体对环境的适应。大多数 OAL 先前的工作都利用刻板的行波步态模板，并依赖局部身体变形（例如，被动身体力学或基于力反馈的分散控制器参数自适应）进行障碍物导航，而 OAL 的步态模板设计研究仍然较少。在本文中，我们基于几何力学 (GM) 衍生的工具探索 OAL 的新颖步态模板，迄今为止该模板仅限于同质环境。在这里，我们将 GM 的范围扩展到障碍丰富的环境。具体来说，我们建立了一个模型，将障碍物的存在映射到优化中的方向约束。在此过程中，我们确定了分别适用于稀疏和密集分布的障碍丰富环境的新颖步态模板。开环机器人物理实验验证了我们确定的 OAL 步态在障碍物丰富的环境中的有效性。我们假设，当这种 OAL 步态模板通过适当的传感和反馈控制得到增强时，无肢运动机将在障碍物丰富的环境中获得强大的功能。

AI.tldr: 本文基于几何力学工具，设计适用于障碍物密集环境的无肢机器人新型运动模式（gait模板），以提升其通过复杂环境的能力。
AI.motivation: 传统无肢机器人依赖波浪式运动模式和局部形变导航障碍物，但缺乏针对障碍物密集环境的系统性gait模板设计，限制了其在复杂场景中的应用。
AI.method: 将几何力学扩展至障碍物环境，建立障碍物与方向约束的优化映射模型，分别设计适用于稀疏和密集障碍物场景的新型gait模板，并通过开环物理实验验证效果。
AI.result: 实验验证了所设计gait模板在障碍物密集环境中的有效性，表明结合感知与反馈控制可显著提升无肢机器人的环境适应能力。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的基于几何力学的gait模板设计方法，为无肢机器人在复杂障碍物环境中的鲁棒运动提供了理论基础与实践验证。

authors_text: Ce Liu,Suryansh Kumar,Shuhang Gu,Radu Timofte,Luc Van Gool
title: VA-DepthNet: A Variational Approach to Single Image Depth Prediction
title_zh: VA-DepthNet：单图像深度预测的变分方法
comment: Accepted for publication at ICLR 2023 (Spotlight Oral Presentation). Draft info: 21 pages, 13 tables, 8 figures
comments: Accepted for publication at ICLR 2023 (Spotlight Oral Presentation). Draft info: 21 pages, 13 tables, 8 figures
abstract: We introduce VA-DepthNet, a simple, effective, and accurate deep neural network approach for the single-image depth prediction (SIDP) problem. The proposed approach advocates using classical first-order variational constraints for this problem. While state-of-the-art deep neural network methods for SIDP learn the scene depth from images in a supervised setting, they often overlook the invaluable invariances and priors in the rigid scene space, such as the regularity of the scene. The paper's main contribution is to reveal the benefit of classical and well-founded variational constraints in the neural network design for the SIDP task. It is shown that imposing first-order variational constraints in the scene space together with popular encoder-decoder-based network architecture design provides excellent results for the supervised SIDP task. The imposed first-order variational constraint makes the network aware of the depth gradient in the scene space, i.e., regularity. The paper demonstrates the usefulness of the proposed approach via extensive evaluation and ablation analysis over several benchmark datasets, such as KITTI, NYU Depth V2, and SUN RGB-D. The VA-DepthNet at test time shows considerable improvements in depth prediction accuracy compared to the prior art and is accurate also at high-frequency regions in the scene space. At the time of writing this paper, our method -- labeled as VA-DepthNet, when tested on the KITTI depth-prediction evaluation set benchmarks, shows state-of-the-art results, and is the top-performing published approach.

The proposed approach advocates using classical first-order variational constraints for this problem. abstract_zh: 我们介绍 VA-DepthNet，这是一种简单、有效且准确的深度神经网络方法，用于解决单图像深度预测（SIDP）问题。所提出的方法主张使用经典的一阶变分约束来解决这个问题。虽然最先进的 SIDP 深度神经网络方法可以在监督环境中从图像中学习场景深度，但它们经常忽略刚性场景空间中宝贵的不变性和先验，例如场景的规律性。本文的主要贡献是揭示了 SIDP 任务的神经网络设计中经典且有充分依据的变分约束的好处。结果表明，在场景空间中施加一阶变分约束以及流行的基于编码器-解码器的网络架构设计为监督 SIDP 任务提供了出色的结果。施加的一阶变分约束使网络意识到场景空间中的深度梯度，即规律性。该论文通过对 KITTI、NYU Depth V2 和 SUN RGB-D 等多个基准数据集的广泛评估和消融分析，证明了所提出方法的实用性。与现有技术相比，VA-DepthNet 在测试时显示出深度预测精度有了相当大的改进，并且在场景空间的高频区域也很准确。在撰写本文时，我们的方法（标记为 VA-DepthNet）在 KITTI 深度预测评估集基准上进行测试时，显示出最先进的结果，并且是表现最好的已发布方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出VA-DepthNet，一种结合变分约束的深度神经网络方法，用于单图像深度预测任务。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习方法在单图像深度预测中忽视了刚性场景空间中的正则性等先验信息，导致高频率区域预测效果不足。
AI.method: 在编码器-解码器架构中引入一阶变分约束，使网络感知场景深度梯度，结合经典变分方法与深度学习框架。
AI.result: 在KITTI、NYU Depth V2等数据集上显著优于现有方法，尤其在高频率区域表现突出，KITTI基准测试达SOTA水平。
AI.conclusion: 经典变分约束与深度学习结合可有效提升单图像深度预测精度，验证了物理先验在神经网络设计中的价值。

title: Do Vision and Language Models Share Concepts? A Vector Space Alignment Study
title_zh: 视觉和语言模型共享概念吗？向量空间对齐研究
comment: 12 pages, long paper accepted by TACL
comments: 12 pages, long paper accepted by TACL
abstract: Large-scale pretrained language models (LMs) are said to ``lack the ability to connect utterances to the world'' (Bender and Koller, 2020), because they do not have ``mental models of the world' '(Mitchell and Krakauer, 2023). If so, one would expect LM representations to be unrelated to representations induced by vision models. We present an empirical evaluation across four families of LMs (BERT, GPT-2, OPT and LLaMA-2) and three vision model architectures (ResNet, SegFormer, and MAE). Our experiments show that LMs partially converge towards representations isomorphic to those of vision models, subject to dispersion, polysemy and frequency. This has important implications for both multi-modal processing and the LM understanding debate (Mitchell and Krakauer, 2023).
summary: Large-scale pretrained language models (LMs) are said to ``lack the ability to connect utterances to the world'' (Bender and Koller, 2020), because they do not have ``mental models of the world' '(Mitchell and Krakauer, 2023). If so, one would expect LM representations to be unrelated to representations induced by vision models. We present an empirical evaluation across four families of LMs (BERT, GPT-2, OPT and LLaMA-2) and three vision model architectures (ResNet, SegFormer, and MAE). Our experiments show that LMs partially converge towards representations isomorphic to those of vision models, subject to dispersion, polysemy and frequency. This has important implications for both multi-modal processing and the LM understanding debate (Mitchell and Krakauer, 2023).
abstract_zh: 大规模预训练语言模型（LM）被认为“缺乏将话语与世界联系起来的能力”（Bender 和 Koller，2020），因为它们没有“世界的心理模型”（Mitchell 和 Krakauer，2023）。如果是这样，人们会期望 LM 表示与视觉模型引起的表示无关。我们对四个 LM 系列（BERT、GPT-2、OPT 和 LLaMA-2）和三种视觉模型架构（ResNet、SegFormer 和 MAE）进行了实证评估。我们的实验表明，语言模型部分地收敛于与视觉模型同构的表示，但受到色散、一词多义和频率的影响。这对于多模态处理和 LM 理解争论都具有重要意义（Mitchell 和 Krakauer，2023）。

AI.tldr: 通过向量空间对齐研究视觉模型与语言模型是否共享概念。
AI.motivation: 验证语言模型是否具备连接语言与世界的能力，挑战其缺乏'世界模型'的现有观点。
AI.method: 对比四类语言模型（BERT等）与三类视觉模型（ResNet等）的表示空间，分析其对齐程度。
AI.result: 语言模型表示在分散性、多义性和频率影响下，部分收敛于视觉模型同构表示。
AI.conclusion: 发现语言模型与视觉模型存在概念重叠，为多模态处理和模型理解提供新视角。

title: Improving robot navigation in crowded environments using intrinsic rewards
title_zh: 使用内在奖励改善拥挤环境中的机器人导航
comment: Paper accepted in 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: Paper accepted in 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: Autonomous navigation in crowded environments is an open problem with many applications, essential for the coexistence of robots and humans in the smart cities of the future. In recent years, deep reinforcement learning approaches have proven to outperform model-based algorithms. Nevertheless, even though the results provided are promising, the works are not able to take advantage of the capabilities that their models offer. They usually get trapped in local optima in the training process, that prevent them from learning the optimal policy. They are not able to visit and interact with every possible state appropriately, such as with the states near the goal or near the dynamic obstacles. In this work, we propose using intrinsic rewards to balance between exploration and exploitation and explore depending on the uncertainty of the states instead of on the time the agent has been trained, encouraging the agent to get more curious about unknown states. We explain the benefits of the approach and compare it with other exploration algorithms that may be used for crowd navigation. Many simulation experiments are performed modifying several algorithms of the state-of-the-art, showing that the use of intrinsic rewards makes the robot learn faster and reach higher rewards and success rates (fewer collisions) in shorter navigation times, outperforming the state-of-the-art.
summary: Autonomous navigation in crowded environments is an open problem with many applications, essential for the coexistence of robots and humans in the smart cities of the future. In recent years, deep reinforcement learning approaches have proven to outperform model-based algorithms. Nevertheless, even though the results provided are promising, the works are not able to take advantage of the capabilities that their models offer. They usually get trapped in local optima in the training process, that prevent them from learning the optimal policy. They are not able to visit and interact with every possible state appropriately, such as with the states near the goal or near the dynamic obstacles. In this work, we propose using intrinsic rewards to balance between exploration and exploitation and explore depending on the uncertainty of the states instead of on the time the agent has been trained, encouraging the agent to get more curious about unknown states. We explain the benefits of the approach and compare it with other exploration algorithms that may be used for crowd navigation. Many simulation experiments are performed modifying several algorithms of the state-of-the-art, showing that the use of intrinsic rewards makes the robot learn faster and reach higher rewards and success rates (fewer collisions) in shorter navigation times, outperforming the state-of-the-art.
abstract_zh: 拥挤环境中的自主导航是许多应用中的一个悬而未决的问题，对于未来智能城市中机器人和人类的共存至关重要。近年来，深度强化学习方法已被证明优于基于模型的算法。然而，尽管提供的结果很有希望，但这些作品无法利用其模型提供的功能。他们通常会在训练过程中陷入局部最优，从而阻止他们学习最优策略。他们无法适当地访问每个可能的状态并与之交互，例如接近目标或接近动态障碍的状态。在这项工作中，我们建议使用内在奖励来平衡探索和利用，并根据状态的不确定性而不是智能体训练的时间进行探索，鼓励智能体对未知状态更加好奇。我们解释了该方法的优点，并将其与可用于人群导航的其他探索算法进行比较。通过修改多种最先​​进的算法进行了许多模拟实验，结果表明，使用内在奖励可以使机器人学习得更快，并在更短的导航时间内获得更高的奖励和成功率（更少的碰撞），优于最先进的算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用内在奖励机制提升机器人在拥挤环境中的自主导航能力。
AI.motivation: 现有深度强化学习方法在拥挤环境导航中易陷入局部最优，无法有效探索关键状态（如目标附近或动态障碍物周围），导致学习效率低下和碰撞率高。
AI.method: 设计基于状态不确定性的内在奖励机制，通过鼓励机器人探索未知状态来平衡探索与利用，替代传统基于训练时长的探索策略。
AI.result: 仿真实验表明，该方法使机器人学习速度提升，成功率提高（碰撞减少），导航时间缩短，优于当前最先进的算法。
AI.conclusion: 内在奖励机制能有效增强机器人在拥挤环境中的探索能力，显著提升导航性能，为未来人机共存场景提供可行方案。

ai_content_json.method: 设计基于状态不确定性的内在奖励机制，通过鼓励机器人探索未知状态来平衡探索与利用，替代传统基于训练时长的探索策略。
ai_content_json.result: 仿真实验表明，该方法使机器人学习速度提升，成功率提高（碰撞减少），导航时间缩短，优于当前最先进的算法。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 内在奖励机制能有效增强机器人在拥挤环境中的探索能力，显著提升导航性能，为未来人机共存场景提供可行方案。
