search_text: Improved Competitive Ratio for Edge-Weighted Online Stochastic Matching 提高边缘加权在线随机匹配的竞争比 We consider the edge-weighted online stochastic matching problem, in which an edge-weighted bipartite graph G=(I\cup J, E) with offline vertices J and online vertex types I is given. The online vertices have types sampled from I with probability proportional to the arrival rates of online vertex types. The online algorithm must make immediate and irrevocable matching decisions with the objective of maximizing the total weight of the matching. For the problem with general arrival rates, Feldman et al. (FOCS 2009) proposed the Suggested Matching algorithm and showed that it achieves a competitive ratio of 1-1/e \approx 0.632. The ratio has recently been improved to 0.645 by Yan (2022), who proposed the Multistage Suggested Matching (MSM) algorithm. In this paper, we propose the Evolving Suggested Matching (ESM) algorithm, and show that it achieves a competitive ratio of 0.650. 我们考虑边加权在线随机匹配问题，其中给出一个具有离线顶点 J 和在线顶点类型 I 的边加权二分图 G=(I\cup J, E)。在线顶点具有从 I 采样的类型，其概率与在线顶点类型的到达率成比例。在线算法必须立即做出不可撤销的匹配决策，以最大化匹配的总权重。对于一般到达率的问题，Feldman 等人。 (FOCS 2009) 提出了建议匹配算法，并表明它实现了 1-1/e \大约 0.632 的竞争比。最近 Yan (2022) 提出了多阶段建议匹配 (MSM) 算法，将该比率提高到 0.645。在本文中，我们提出了进化建议匹配（ESM）算法，并表明它的竞争比达到了 0.650。 本文针对边权重在线随机匹配问题，提出改进的算法以提高竞争比。 Yilong Feng,Guoliang Qiu,Xiaowei Wu,Shengwei Zhou cs.DS

title: Operation-level Progressive Differentiable Architecture Search
title_zh: 操作级渐进式可微架构搜索
abstract: Differentiable Neural Architecture Search (DARTS) is becoming more and more popular among Neural Architecture Search (NAS) methods because of its high search efficiency and low compute cost. However, the stability of DARTS is very inferior, especially skip connections aggregation that leads to performance collapse. Though existing methods leverage Hessian eigenvalues to alleviate skip connections aggregation, they make DARTS unable to explore architectures with better performance. In the paper, we propose operation-level progressive differentiable neural architecture search (OPP-DARTS) to avoid skip connections aggregation and explore better architectures simultaneously. We first divide the search process into several stages during the search phase and increase candidate operations into the search space progressively at the beginning of each stage. It can effectively alleviate the unfair competition between operations during the search phase of DARTS by offsetting the inherent unfair advantage of the skip connection over other operations. Besides, to keep the competition between operations relatively fair and select the operation from the candidate operations set that makes training loss of the supernet largest. The experiment results indicate that our method is effective and efficient. Our method's performance on CIFAR-10 is superior to the architecture found by standard DARTS, and the transferability of our method also surpasses standard DARTS. We further demonstrate the robustness of our method on three simple search spaces, i.e., S2, S3, S4, and the results show us that our method is more robust than standard DARTS. Our code is available at https://github.com/zxunyu/OPP-DARTS.
summary: Differentiable Neural Architecture Search (DARTS) is becoming more and more popular among Neural Architecture Search (NAS) methods because of its high search efficiency and low compute cost. However, the stability of DARTS is very inferior, especially skip connections aggregation that leads to performance collapse. Though existing methods leverage Hessian eigenvalues to alleviate skip connections aggregation, they make DARTS unable to explore architectures with better performance. In the paper, we propose operation-level progressive differentiable neural architecture search (OPP-DARTS) to avoid skip connections aggregation and explore better architectures simultaneously. We first divide the search process into several stages during the search phase and increase candidate operations into the search space progressively at the beginning of each stage. It can effectively alleviate the unfair competition between operations during the search phase of DARTS by offsetting the inherent unfair advantage of the skip connection over other operations. Besides, to keep the competition between operations relatively fair and select the operation from the candidate operations set that makes training loss of the supernet largest. The experiment results indicate that our method is effective and efficient. Our method's performance on CIFAR-10 is superior to the architecture found by standard DARTS, and the transferability of our method also surpasses standard DARTS. We further demonstrate the robustness of our method on three simple search spaces, i.e., S2, S3, S4, and the results show us that our method is more robust than standard DARTS. Our code is available at https://github.com/zxunyu/OPP-DARTS.
abstract_zh: 可微神经架构搜索（DARTS）因其高搜索效率和低计算成本而在神经架构搜索（NAS）方法中变得越来越流行。但DARTS的稳定性非常差，尤其是跳过连接聚合导致性能崩溃。尽管现有方法利用 Hessian 特征值来减轻跳跃连接聚合，但它们使得 DARTS 无法探索具有更好性能的架构。在本文中，我们提出了操作级渐进可微神经架构搜索（OPP-DARTS），以避免跳过连接聚合并同时探索更好的架构。我们首先在搜索阶段将搜索过程分为几个阶段，并在每个阶段开始时逐步将候选操作添加到搜索空间中。通过抵消跳跃连接相对于其他操作固有的不公平优势，可以有效缓解DARTS搜索阶段操作之间的不公平竞争。此外，为了保持操作之间的竞争相对公平，并从候选操作集中选择使超网训练损失最大的操作。实验结果表明我们的方法是有效且高效的。我们的方法在 CIFAR-10 上的性能优于标准 DARTS 的架构，并且我们方法的可移植性也超过了标准 DARTS。我们进一步证明了我们的方法在三个简单搜索空间（即 S2、S3、S4）上的鲁棒性，结果表明我们的方法比标准 DARTS 更鲁棒。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/zxunyu/OPP-DARTS 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种操作级渐进式可微分神经架构搜索方法（OPP-DARTS），旨在解决DARTS在搜索过程中因跳过连接聚合导致的稳定性问题，并提升架构搜索性能。
AI.motivation: 现有的DARTS方法因跳过连接的聚合效应导致性能崩溃，虽有方法通过海森矩阵特征值缓解问题，但限制了对更优架构的探索，亟需一种稳定且高效的架构搜索方案。
AI.method: 将搜索过程分为多个阶段，在每个阶段初期逐步向搜索空间中增加候选操作，通过缓解跳过连接的固有优势来平衡操作间的竞争，并选择使超网络训练损失最大的操作。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10数据集上，OPP-DARTS的性能优于标准DARTS，且迁移能力更优；在S2、S3、S4三个简单搜索空间中验证了方法的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: OPP-DARTS通过渐进式操作引入和竞争平衡机制，有效提升了DARTS的稳定性与搜索性能，是一种更高效且鲁棒的神经架构搜索方法。

authors_text: Deyu Bo,Xiao Wang,Yang Liu,Yuan Fang,Yawen Li,Chuan Shi
title: A Survey on Spectral Graph Neural Networks
title_zh: 谱图神经网络综述
abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have attracted considerable attention from the research community. It is well established that GNNs are usually roughly divided into spatial and spectral methods. Despite that spectral GNNs play an important role in both graph signal processing and graph representation learning, existing studies are biased toward spatial approaches, and there is no comprehensive review on spectral GNNs so far. In this paper, we summarize the recent development of spectral GNNs, including model, theory, and application. Specifically, we first discuss the connection between spatial GNNs and spectral GNNs, which shows that spectral GNNs can capture global information and have better expressiveness and interpretability. Next, we categorize existing spectral GNNs according to the spectrum information they use, \ie, eigenvalues or eigenvectors. In addition, we review major theoretical results and applications of spectral GNNs, followed by a quantitative experiment to benchmark some popular spectral GNNs. Finally, we conclude the paper with some future directions.
summary: Graph neural networks (GNNs) have attracted considerable attention from the research community. It is well established that GNNs are usually roughly divided into spatial and spectral methods. Despite that spectral GNNs play an important role in both graph signal processing and graph representation learning, existing studies are biased toward spatial approaches, and there is no comprehensive review on spectral GNNs so far. In this paper, we summarize the recent development of spectral GNNs, including model, theory, and application. Specifically, we first discuss the connection between spatial GNNs and spectral GNNs, which shows that spectral GNNs can capture global information and have better expressiveness and interpretability. Next, we categorize existing spectral GNNs according to the spectrum information they use, \ie, eigenvalues or eigenvectors. In addition, we review major theoretical results and applications of spectral GNNs, followed by a quantitative experiment to benchmark some popular spectral GNNs. Finally, we conclude the paper with some future directions.
abstract_zh: 图神经网络（GNN）引起了研究界的广泛关注。众所周知，GNN 通常大致分为空间方法和谱方法。尽管谱GNN在图信号处理和图表示学习中都发挥着重要作用，但现有研究偏向于空间方法，并且迄今为止还没有对谱GNN的全面综述。在本文中，我们总结了谱 GNN 的最新发展，包括模型、理论和应用。具体来说，我们首先讨论空间 GNN 和谱 GNN 之间的联系，这表明谱 GNN 可以捕获全局信息，并具有更好的表达性和可解释性。接下来，我们根据现有的频谱 GNN 使用的频谱信息（即特征值或特征向量）对它们进行分类。此外，我们回顾了谱 GNN 的主要理论成果和应用，然后进行了定量实验来对一些流行的谱 GNN 进行基准测试。最后，我们总结了本文的一些未来方向。

AI.tldr: 本文对谱图神经网络（Spectral GNNs）的最新发展进行了全面综述，涵盖模型、理论和应用。
AI.motivation: 尽管谱GNNs在图信号处理和图表示学习中具有重要作用，但现有研究更偏向于空间方法，缺乏对谱GNNs的系统性回顾。
AI.method: 通过分析谱GNNs与空间GNNs的联系，按谱信息（特征值或特征向量）分类现有方法，并综述理论结果、应用场景及定量实验对比。
AI.result: 总结了谱GNNs的模型架构与理论基础，验证了其全局信息捕捉能力，并通过实验基准测试了主流方法的性能。
AI.conclusion: 指出现阶段谱GNNs的研究方向，包括理论深化、跨领域应用拓展及与空间方法的融合潜力。

title: Improving Differentiable Architecture Search via Self-Distillation
title_zh: 通过自蒸馏改进可微架构搜索
abstract: Differentiable Architecture Search (DARTS) is a simple yet efficient Neural Architecture Search (NAS) method. During the search stage, DARTS trains a supernet by jointly optimizing architecture parameters and network parameters. During the evaluation stage, DARTS discretizes the supernet to derive the optimal architecture based on architecture parameters. However, recent research has shown that during the training process, the supernet tends to converge towards sharp minima rather than flat minima. This is evidenced by the higher sharpness of the loss landscape of the supernet, which ultimately leads to a performance gap between the supernet and the optimal architecture. In this paper, we propose Self-Distillation Differentiable Neural Architecture Search (SD-DARTS) to alleviate the discretization gap. We utilize self-distillation to distill knowledge from previous steps of the supernet to guide its training in the current step, effectively reducing the sharpness of the supernet's loss and bridging the performance gap between the supernet and the optimal architecture. Furthermore, we introduce the concept of voting teachers, where multiple previous supernets are selected as teachers, and their output probabilities are aggregated through voting to obtain the final teacher prediction. Experimental results on real datasets demonstrate the advantages of our novel self-distillation-based NAS method compared to state-of-the-art alternatives.
summary: Differentiable Architecture Search (DARTS) is a simple yet efficient Neural Architecture Search (NAS) method. During the search stage, DARTS trains a supernet by jointly optimizing architecture parameters and network parameters. During the evaluation stage, DARTS discretizes the supernet to derive the optimal architecture based on architecture parameters. However, recent research has shown that during the training process, the supernet tends to converge towards sharp minima rather than flat minima. This is evidenced by the higher sharpness of the loss landscape of the supernet, which ultimately leads to a performance gap between the supernet and the optimal architecture. In this paper, we propose Self-Distillation Differentiable Neural Architecture Search (SD-DARTS) to alleviate the discretization gap. We utilize self-distillation to distill knowledge from previous steps of the supernet to guide its training in the current step, effectively reducing the sharpness of the supernet's loss and bridging the performance gap between the supernet and the optimal architecture. Furthermore, we introduce the concept of voting teachers, where multiple previous supernets are selected as teachers, and their output probabilities are aggregated through voting to obtain the final teacher prediction. Experimental results on real datasets demonstrate the advantages of our novel self-distillation-based NAS method compared to state-of-the-art alternatives.
abstract_zh: 可微架构搜索（DARTS）是一种简单而高效的神经架构搜索（NAS）方法。在搜索阶段，DARTS 通过联合优化架构参数和网络参数来训练超网。在评估阶段，DARTS 对超网进行离散化，以根据架构参数得出最佳架构。然而，最近的研究表明，在训练过程中，超网倾向于收敛到尖锐的最小值而不是平坦的最小值。超网的损失景观的锐度更高就证明了这一点，这最终导致了超网与最优架构之间的性能差距。在本文中，我们提出自蒸馏可微神经架构搜索（SD-DARTS）来缓解离散化差距。我们利用自蒸馏从超网先前步骤中提取知识来指导其当前步骤的训练，有效降低了超网损失的锐度，并弥合了超网与最优架构之间的性能差距。此外，我们引入了投票教师的概念，即选择多个先前的超网作为教师，并通过投票聚合它们的输出概率以获得最终的教师预测。真实数据集上的实验结果证明了我们新颖的基于自蒸馏的 NAS 方法与最先进的替代方法相比的优势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于自蒸馏的微分神经架构搜索方法（SD-DARTS），旨在缓解DARTS中因超网络收敛至尖锐最小值导致的性能差距问题。
AI.motivation: DARTS在训练过程中超网络倾向于收敛到尖锐最小值，导致超网络与最终离散化架构之间存在显著性能差距。
AI.method: 通过自蒸馏机制将超网络前期步骤的知识传递至当前训练步骤，并引入多个历史超网络作为投票教师，聚合其输出概率以指导当前训练。
AI.result: 在真实数据集上的实验表明，SD-DARTS相比现有先进方法有效缩小了超网络与最优架构的性能差距。
AI.conclusion: 自蒸馏与投票教师机制的结合能够降低超网络损失曲面的尖锐性，从而提升DARTS的搜索效果与最终架构性能。

We utilize self-distillation to distill knowledge from previous steps of the supernet to guide its training in the current step, effectively reducing the sharpness of the supernet's loss and bridging the performance gap between the supernet and the optimal architecture. Furthermore, we introduce the concept of voting teachers, where multiple previous supernets are selected as teachers, and their output probabilities are aggregated through voting to obtain the final teacher prediction. title: A novel approach to generate datasets with XAI ground truth to evaluate image models
title_zh: 一种使用 XAI 地面实况生成数据集以评估图像模型的新方法
abstract: With the increased usage of artificial intelligence (AI), it is imperative to understand how these models work internally. These needs have led to the development of a new field called eXplainable artificial intelligence (XAI). This field consists of on a set of techniques that allows us to theoretically determine the cause of the AI decisions. One main issue of XAI is how to verify the works on this field, taking into consideration the lack of ground truth (GT). In this study, we propose a new method to generate datasets with GT. We conducted a set of experiments that compared our GT with real model explanations and obtained excellent results confirming that our proposed method is correct.
summary: With the increased usage of artificial intelligence (AI), it is imperative to understand how these models work internally. These needs have led to the development of a new field called eXplainable artificial intelligence (XAI). This field consists of on a set of techniques that allows us to theoretically determine the cause of the AI decisions. One main issue of XAI is how to verify the works on this field, taking into consideration the lack of ground truth (GT). In this study, we propose a new method to generate datasets with GT. We conducted a set of experiments that compared our GT with real model explanations and obtained excellent results confirming that our proposed method is correct.
abstract_zh: 随着人工智能 (AI) 使用的增加，了解这些模型的内部工作原理势在必行。这些需求催生了一个名为可解释人工智能（XAI）的新领域的发展。该领域由一组技术组成，使我们能够从理论上确定人工智能决策的原因。 XAI 的一个主要问题是如何验证这一领域的工作，同时考虑到缺乏地面实况（GT）。在本研究中，我们提出了一种使用 GT 生成数据集的新方法。我们进行了一系列实验，将我们的 GT 与真实模型解释进行比较，并获得了出色的结果，证实了我们提出的方法是正确的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种生成带有XAI真实标注数据集的新方法，用于评估图像模型的可解释性。
AI.motivation: 随着AI应用的普及，理解模型内部决策机制的需求增加，但现有XAI技术缺乏真实标注数据来验证其有效性。
AI.method: 设计生成包含可解释性标注的数据集的方法，并通过对比实验验证生成的标注与真实模型解释的一致性。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，生成的标注与实际模型解释高度吻合，验证了方法的准确性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了XAI领域标注数据不足的问题，为模型可解释性研究提供了可靠的数据基础。

authors_text: Reza Hadi Mogavi,Chao Deng,Jennifer Hoffman,Ehsan-Ul Haq,Sujit Gujar,Antonio Bucchiarone,Pan Hui
title: Your Favorite Gameplay Speaks Volumes about You: Predicting User Behavior and Hexad Type
title_zh: 您最喜欢的游戏玩法足以说明您的情况：预测用户行为和 Hexad 类型
comment: This manuscript is the pre-print version of our paper accepted at the conference of HCI International 2023
comments: This manuscript is the pre-print version of our paper accepted at the conference of HCI International 2023
abstract: In recent years, the gamification research community has widely and frequently questioned the effectiveness of one-size-fits-all gamification schemes. In consequence, personalization seems to be an important part of any successful gamification design. Personalization can be improved by understanding user behavior and Hexad player/user type. This paper comes with an original research idea: It investigates whether users' game-related data (collected via various gamer-archetype surveys) can be used to predict their behavioral characteristics and Hexad user types in non-game (but gamified) contexts. The affinity that exists between the concepts of gamification and gaming provided us with the impetus for running this exploratory research. We conducted an initial survey study with 67 Stack Exchange users (as a case study). We discovered that users' gameplay information could reveal valuable and helpful information about their behavioral characteristics and Hexad user types in a non-gaming (but gamified) environment. The results of testing three gamer archetypes (i.e., Bartle, Big Five, and BrainHex) show that they can all help predict users' most dominant Stack Exchange behavioral characteristics and Hexad user type better than a random labeler's baseline. That said, of all the gamer archetypes analyzed in this paper, BrainHex performs the best. In the end, we introduce a research agenda for future work.
summary: In recent years, the gamification research community has widely and frequently questioned the effectiveness of one-size-fits-all gamification schemes. In consequence, personalization seems to be an important part of any successful gamification design. Personalization can be improved by understanding user behavior and Hexad player/user type. This paper comes with an original research idea: It investigates whether users' game-related data (collected via various gamer-archetype surveys) can be used to predict their behavioral characteristics and Hexad user types in non-game (but gamified) contexts. The affinity that exists between the concepts of gamification and gaming provided us with the impetus for running this exploratory research. We conducted an initial survey study with 67 Stack Exchange users (as a case study). We discovered that users' gameplay information could reveal valuable and helpful information about their behavioral characteristics and Hexad user types in a non-gaming (but gamified) environment. The results of testing three gamer archetypes (i.e., Bartle, Big Five, and BrainHex) show that they can all help predict users' most dominant Stack Exchange behavioral characteristics and Hexad user type better than a random labeler's baseline. That said, of all the gamer archetypes analyzed in this paper, BrainHex performs the best. In the end, we introduce a research agenda for future work.
abstract_zh: 近年来，游戏化研究界广泛而频繁地质疑一刀切的游戏化方案的有效性。因此，个性化似乎是任何成功的游戏化设计的重要组成部分。通过了解用户行为和 Hexad 玩家/用户类型可以改进个性化。本文提出了一个原创的研究想法：研究用户的游戏相关数据（通过各种游戏玩家原型调查收集）是否可以用于预测他们在非游戏（但游戏化）环境中的行为特征和 Hexad 用户类型。游戏化和游戏概念之间存在的密切关系为我们进行这项探索性研究提供了动力。我们对 67 位 Stack Exchange 用户进行了初步调查研究（作为案例研究）。我们发现，用户的游戏信息可以揭示有关他们在非游戏（但游戏化）环境中的行为特征和 Hexad 用户类型的有价值且有用的信息。测试三种游戏玩家原型（即 Bartle、Big Five 和 BrainHex）的结果表明，它们都可以比随机标记者的基线更好地帮助预测用户最主要的 Stack Exchange 行为特征和 Hexad 用户类型。也就是说，在本文分析的所有玩家原型中，BrainHex 表现最好。最后，我们介绍了未来工作的研究议程。

AI.tldr: 本文研究通过分析用户的游戏行为数据，预测其在非游戏化场景中的行为特征和Hexad用户类型。
AI.motivation: 传统统一化的游戏化方案效果有限，需通过个性化设计提升效果，而理解用户行为和Hexad类型是关键。
AI.method: 对67名Stack Exchange用户进行调查，分析三种游戏用户模型（Bartle、Big Five、BrainHex）预测行为特征的能力。
AI.result: 三种模型均优于随机预测，其中BrainHex在预测用户行为特征和Hexad类型上表现最佳。
AI.conclusion: 提出未来研究方向，进一步探索游戏行为数据与非游戏化场景用户特征的关联性。

motivation: 传统统一化的游戏化方案效果有限，需通过个性化设计提升效果，而理解用户行为和Hexad类型是关键。
method: 对67名Stack Exchange用户进行调查，分析三种游戏用户模型（Bartle、Big Five、BrainHex）预测行为特征的能力。
result: 三种模型均优于随机预测，其中BrainHex在预测用户行为特征和Hexad类型上表现最佳。
conclusion: 提出未来研究方向，进一步探索游戏行为数据与非游戏化场景用户特征的关联性。
search_text: Your Favorite Gameplay Speaks Volumes about You: Predicting User Behavior and Hexad Type 您最喜欢的游戏玩法足以说明您的情况：预测用户行为和 Hexad 类型 In recent years, the gamification research community has widely and frequently questioned the effectiveness of one-size-fits-all gamification schemes. In consequence, personalization seems to be an important part of any successful gamification design. Personalization can be improved by understanding user behavior and Hexad player/user type. This paper comes with an original research idea: It investigates whether users' game-related data (collected via various gamer-archetype surveys) can be used to predict their behavioral characteristics and Hexad user types in non-game (but gamified) contexts. The affinity that exists between the concepts of gamification and gaming provided us with the impetus for running this exploratory research. We conducted an initial survey study with 67 Stack Exchange users (as a case study). We discovered that users' gameplay information could reveal valuable and helpful information about their behavioral characteristics and Hexad user types in a non-gaming (but gamified) environment. The results of testing three gamer archetypes (i.e., Bartle, Big Five, and BrainHex) show that they can all help predict users' most dominant Stack Exchange behavioral characteristics and Hexad user type better than a random labeler's baseline. That said, of all the gamer archetypes analyzed in this paper, BrainHex performs the best. In the end, we introduce a research agenda for future work. 近年来，游戏化研究界广泛而频繁地质疑一刀切的游戏化方案的有效性。因此，个性化似乎是任何成功的游戏化设计的重要组成部分。通过了解用户行为和 Hexad 玩家/用户类型可以改进个性化。本文提出了一个原创的研究想法：研究用户的游戏相关数据（通过各种游戏玩家原型调查收集）是否可以用于预测他们在非游戏（但游戏化）环境中的行为特征和 Hexad 用户类型。游戏化和游戏概念之间存在的密切关系为我们进行这项探索性研究提供了动力。我们对 67 位 Stack Exchange 用户进行了初步调查研究（作为案例研究）。我们发现，用户的游戏信息可以揭示有关他们在非游戏（但游戏化）环境中的行为特征和 Hexad 用户类型的有价值且有用的信息。测试三种游戏玩家原型（即 Bartle、Big Five 和 BrainHex）的结果表明，它们都可以比随机标记者的基线更好地帮助预测用户最主要的 Stack Exchange 行为特征和 Hexad 用户类型。也就是说，在本文分析的所有玩家原型中，BrainHex 表现最好。最后，我们介绍了未来工作的研究议程。 本文研究通过分析用户的游戏行为数据，预测其在非游戏化场景中的行为特征和Hexad用户类型。 Reza Hadi Mogavi,Chao Deng,Jennifer Hoffman,Ehsan-Ul Haq,Sujit Gujar,Antonio Bucchiarone,Pan Hui cs.HC

authors_text: Xu Ling,Yichen Lu,Wenqi Xu,Weihong Deng,Yingjie Zhang,Xingchen Cui,Hongzhi Shi,Dongchao Wen
title: Dive into the Resolution Augmentations and Metrics in Low Resolution Face Recognition: A Plain yet Effective New Baseline
title_zh: 深入研究低分辨率人脸识别中的分辨率增强和指标：简单而有效的新基准
abstract: Although deep learning has significantly improved Face Recognition (FR), dramatic performance deterioration may occur when processing Low Resolution (LR) faces. To alleviate this, approaches based on unified feature space are proposed with the sacrifice under High Resolution (HR) circumstances. To deal with the huge domain gap between HR and LR domains and achieve the best on both domains, we first took a closer look at the impacts of several resolution augmentations and then analyzed the difficulty of LR samples from the perspective of the model gradient produced by different resolution samples. Besides, we also find that the introduction of some resolutions could help the learning of lower resolutions. Based on these, we divide the LR samples into three difficulties according to the resolution and propose a more effective Multi-Resolution Augmentation. Then, due to the rapidly increasing domain gap as the resolution decreases, we carefully design a novel and effective metric loss based on a LogExp distance function that provides decent gradients to prevent oscillation near the convergence point or tolerance to small distance errors; it could also dynamically adjust the penalty for errors in different dimensions, allowing for more optimization of dimensions with large errors. Combining these two insights, our model could learn more general knowledge in a wide resolution range of images and balanced results can be achieved by our extremely simple framework. Moreover, the augmentations and metrics are the cornerstones of LRFR, so our method could be considered a new baseline for the LRFR task. Experiments on the LRFR datasets: SCface, XQLFW, and large-scale LRFR dataset: TinyFace demonstrate the effectiveness of our methods, while the degradation on HRFR datasets is significantly reduced.
summary: Although deep learning has significantly improved Face Recognition (FR), dramatic performance deterioration may occur when processing Low Resolution (LR) faces. To alleviate this, approaches based on unified feature space are proposed with the sacrifice under High Resolution (HR) circumstances. To deal with the huge domain gap between HR and LR domains and achieve the best on both domains, we first took a closer look at the impacts of several resolution augmentations and then analyzed the difficulty of LR samples from the perspective of the model gradient produced by different resolution samples. Besides, we also find that the introduction of some resolutions could help the learning of lower resolutions. Based on these, we divide the LR samples into three difficulties according to the resolution and propose a more effective Multi-Resolution Augmentation. Then, due to the rapidly increasing domain gap as the resolution decreases, we carefully design a novel and effective metric loss based on a LogExp distance function that provides decent gradients to prevent oscillation near the convergence point or tolerance to small distance errors; it could also dynamically adjust the penalty for errors in different dimensions, allowing for more optimization of dimensions with large errors. Combining these two insights, our model could learn more general knowledge in a wide resolution range of images and balanced results can be achieved by our extremely simple framework. Moreover, the augmentations and metrics are the cornerstones of LRFR, so our method could be considered a new baseline for the LRFR task. Experiments on the LRFR datasets: SCface, XQLFW, and large-scale LRFR dataset: TinyFace demonstrate the effectiveness of our methods, while the degradation on HRFR datasets is significantly reduced.
abstract_zh: 尽管深度学习显着改善了人脸识别（FR），但在处理低分辨率（LR）人脸时可能会出现性能急剧下降。为了缓解这个问题，提出了基于统一特征空间的方法，但牺牲了高分辨率（HR）环境。为了解决 HR 和 LR 领域之间巨大的领域差距并在两个领域都取得最佳效果，我们首先仔细研究了几种分辨率增强的影响，然后从不同分辨率样本产生的模型梯度的角度分析了 LR 样本的难度。此外，我们还发现引入一些分辨率可以帮助学习较低分辨率。在此基础上，我们根据分辨率将LR样本分为三个难点，并提出了更有效的多分辨率增强。然后，由于域间隙随着分辨率的降低而迅速增加，我们精心设计了一种基于 LogExp 距离函数的新颖且有效的度量损失，该函数提供适当的梯度以防止收敛点附近的振荡或对小距离误差的容忍；它还可以动态调整不同维度上错误的惩罚，从而可以对错误较大的维度进行更多优化。结合这两种见解，我们的模型可以在广泛的分辨率范围的图像中学习更多的常识，并且可以通过我们极其简单的框架来实现平衡的结果。此外，增强和度量是 LRFR 的基石，因此我们的方法可以被视为 LRFR 任务的新基线。在LRFR数据集SCface、XQLFW和大规模LRFR数据集TinyFace上的实验证明了我们方法的有效性，同时HRFR数据集上的退化显着减少。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种针对低分辨率人脸识别（LRFR）的新型基准方法，通过分辨率增强和度量损失设计提升模型在高低分辨率场景下的性能。
AI.motivation: 深度学习在高分辨率人脸识别中表现优异，但低分辨率场景下性能显著下降，且现有方法在提升低分辨率性能时可能损害高分辨率表现，亟需兼顾两者的有效解决方案。
AI.method: 通过分析不同分辨率增强对模型梯度的影响，将低分辨率样本按难度分为三类并设计多分辨率增强策略；提出基于LogExp距离函数的动态度量损失，以平衡不同维度的误差惩罚并优化收敛稳定性。
AI.result: 在SCface、XQLFW和TinyFace等数据集上验证了方法的有效性，显著提升低分辨率识别性能且减少高分辨率场景的性能退化。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的分辨率增强策略与度量损失设计为LRFR任务提供了简洁高效的新型基准，其核心思想对相关领域具有重要参考价值。

title: Evaluating the Robustness of Discrete Prompts
title_zh: 评估离散提示的稳健性
abstract: Discrete prompts have been used for fine-tuning Pre-trained Language Models for diverse NLP tasks. In particular, automatic methods that generate discrete prompts from a small set of training instances have reported superior performance. However, a closer look at the learnt prompts reveals that they contain noisy and counter-intuitive lexical constructs that would not be encountered in manually-written prompts. This raises an important yet understudied question regarding the robustness of automatically learnt discrete prompts when used in downstream tasks. To address this question, we conduct a systematic study of the robustness of discrete prompts by applying carefully designed perturbations into an application using AutoPrompt and then measure their performance in two Natural Language Inference (NLI) datasets. Our experimental results show that although the discrete prompt-based method remains relatively robust against perturbations to NLI inputs, they are highly sensitive to other types of perturbations such as shuffling and deletion of prompt tokens. Moreover, they generalize poorly across different NLI datasets. We hope our findings will inspire future work on robust discrete prompt learning.
summary: Discrete prompts have been used for fine-tuning Pre-trained Language Models for diverse NLP tasks. In particular, automatic methods that generate discrete prompts from a small set of training instances have reported superior performance. However, a closer look at the learnt prompts reveals that they contain noisy and counter-intuitive lexical constructs that would not be encountered in manually-written prompts. This raises an important yet understudied question regarding the robustness of automatically learnt discrete prompts when used in downstream tasks. To address this question, we conduct a systematic study of the robustness of discrete prompts by applying carefully designed perturbations into an application using AutoPrompt and then measure their performance in two Natural Language Inference (NLI) datasets. Our experimental results show that although the discrete prompt-based method remains relatively robust against perturbations to NLI inputs, they are highly sensitive to other types of perturbations such as shuffling and deletion of prompt tokens. Moreover, they generalize poorly across different NLI datasets. We hope our findings will inspire future work on robust discrete prompt learning.
abstract_zh: 离散提示已用于针对各种 NLP 任务微调预训练语言模型。特别是，从一小组训练实例生成离散提示的自动方法已经报告了卓越的性能。然而，仔细观察学习的提示会发现，它们包含嘈杂且反直觉的词汇结构，而这些在手动编写的提示中不会遇到。这就提出了一个重要但尚未充分研究的问题，即自动学习的离散提示在下游任务中使用时的鲁棒性。为了解决这个问题，我们通过使用 AutoPrompt 将精心设计的扰动应用到应用程序中，对离散提示的鲁棒性进行了系统研究，然后在两个自然语言推理 (NLI) 数据集中测量其性能。我们的实验结果表明，尽管基于离散提示的方法对于 NLI 输入的扰动仍然相对稳健，但它们对其他类型的扰动（例如提示标记的改组和删除）高度敏感。此外，它们在不同 NLI 数据集上的泛化能力很差。我们希望我们的发现能够激发未来关于稳健离散即时学习的工作。

AI.tldr: 本文评估自动学习的离散提示在下游自然语言推理任务中的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 自动生成的离散提示虽有效，但包含噪音和反直觉结构，其鲁棒性缺乏系统研究。
AI.method: 通过AutoPrompt应用精心设计的扰动（如输入修改、提示词洗牌/删除），在两个NLI数据集上测试性能。
AI.result: 离散提示对输入扰动较鲁棒，但对提示结构扰动敏感，且跨数据集泛化能力差。
AI.conclusion: 需进一步研究提升离散提示学习的鲁棒性和跨任务泛化能力。

search_text: Evaluating the Robustness of Discrete Prompts 评估离散提示的稳健性 Discrete prompts have been used for fine-tuning Pre-trained Language Models for diverse NLP tasks. In particular, automatic methods that generate discrete prompts from a small set of training instances have reported superior performance. However, a closer look at the learnt prompts reveals that they contain noisy and counter-intuitive lexical constructs that would not be encountered in manually-written prompts. This raises an important yet understudied question regarding the robustness of automatically learnt discrete prompts when used in downstream tasks. To address this question, we conduct a systematic study of the robustness of discrete prompts by applying carefully designed perturbations into an application using AutoPrompt and then measure their performance in two Natural Language Inference (NLI) datasets. Our experimental results show that although the discrete prompt-based method remains relatively robust against perturbations to NLI inputs, they are highly sensitive to other types of perturbations such as shuffling and deletion of prompt tokens. Moreover, they generalize poorly across different NLI datasets. We hope our findings will inspire future work on robust discrete prompt learning. 离散提示已用于针对各种 NLP 任务微调预训练语言模型。特别是，从一小组训练实例生成离散提示的自动方法已经报告了卓越的性能。然而，仔细观察学习的提示会发现，它们包含嘈杂且反直觉的词汇结构，而这些在手动编写的提示中不会遇到。这就提出了一个重要但尚未充分研究的问题，即自动学习的离散提示在下游任务中使用时的鲁棒性。为了解决这个问题，我们通过使用 AutoPrompt 将精心设计的扰动应用到应用程序中，对离散提示的鲁棒性进行了系统研究，然后在两个自然语言推理 (NLI) 数据集中测量其性能。我们的实验结果表明，尽管基于离散提示的方法对于 NLI 输入的扰动仍然相对稳健，但它们对其他类型的扰动（例如提示标记的改组和删除）高度敏感。此外，它们在不同 NLI 数据集上的泛化能力很差。我们希望我们的发现能够激发未来关于稳健离散即时学习的工作。 本文评估自动学习的离散提示在下游自然语言推理任务中的鲁棒性。 Yoichi Ishibashi,Danushka Bollegala,Katsuhito Sudoh,Satoshi Nakamura cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Yankai Jiang,Mingze Sun,Heng Guo,Xiaoyu Bai,Ke Yan,Le Lu,Minfeng Xu
title: Anatomical Invariance Modeling and Semantic Alignment for Self-supervised Learning in 3D Medical Image Analysis
title_zh: 3D 医学图像分析中自监督学习的解剖不变性建模和语义对齐
comment: This paper has been accepted by ICCV 2023 (oral)
comments: This paper has been accepted by ICCV 2023 (oral)
abstract: Self-supervised learning (SSL) has recently achieved promising performance for 3D medical image analysis tasks. Most current methods follow existing SSL paradigm originally designed for photographic or natural images, which cannot explicitly and thoroughly exploit the intrinsic similar anatomical structures across varying medical images. This may in fact degrade the quality of learned deep representations by maximizing the similarity among features containing spatial misalignment information and different anatomical semantics. In this work, we propose a new self-supervised learning framework, namely Alice, that explicitly fulfills Anatomical invariance modeling and semantic alignment via elaborately combining discriminative and generative objectives. Alice introduces a new contrastive learning strategy which encourages the similarity between views that are diversely mined but with consistent high-level semantics, in order to learn invariant anatomical features. Moreover, we design a conditional anatomical feature alignment module to complement corrupted embeddings with globally matched semantics and inter-patch topology information, conditioned by the distribution of local image content, which permits to create better contrastive pairs. Our extensive quantitative experiments on three 3D medical image analysis tasks demonstrate and validate the performance superiority of Alice, surpassing the previous best SSL counterpart methods and showing promising ability for united representation learning. Codes are available at https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/alice.
summary: Self-supervised learning (SSL) has recently achieved promising performance for 3D medical image analysis tasks. Most current methods follow existing SSL paradigm originally designed for photographic or natural images, which cannot explicitly and thoroughly exploit the intrinsic similar anatomical structures across varying medical images. This may in fact degrade the quality of learned deep representations by maximizing the similarity among features containing spatial misalignment information and different anatomical semantics. In this work, we propose a new self-supervised learning framework, namely Alice, that explicitly fulfills Anatomical invariance modeling and semantic alignment via elaborately combining discriminative and generative objectives. Alice introduces a new contrastive learning strategy which encourages the similarity between views that are diversely mined but with consistent high-level semantics, in order to learn invariant anatomical features. Moreover, we design a conditional anatomical feature alignment module to complement corrupted embeddings with globally matched semantics and inter-patch topology information, conditioned by the distribution of local image content, which permits to create better contrastive pairs. Our extensive quantitative experiments on three 3D medical image analysis tasks demonstrate and validate the performance superiority of Alice, surpassing the previous best SSL counterpart methods and showing promising ability for united representation learning. Codes are available at https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/alice.
abstract_zh: 自监督学习 (SSL) 最近在 3D 医学图像分析任务中取得了令人鼓舞的性能。目前的大多数方法都遵循最初为摄影或自然图像设计的现有 SSL 范式，这些范式无法明确且彻底地利用不同医学图像之间内在相似的解剖结构。事实上，这可能会通过最大化包含空间错位信息和不同解剖语义的特征之间的相似性来降低学习的深度表示的质量。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新的自我监督学习框架，即 Alice，它通过精心结合判别目标和生成目标来明确实现解剖不变性建模和语义对齐。 Alice 引入了一种新的对比学习策略，该策略鼓励不同挖掘但具有一致的高级语义的视图之间的相似性，以便学习不变的解剖特征。此外，我们设计了一个条件解剖特征对齐模块，以全局匹配的语义和块间拓扑信息来补充损坏的嵌入，以局部图像内容的分布为条件，这允许创建更好的对比对。我们对三个 3D 医学图像分析任务进行了广泛的定量实验，证明并验证了 Alice 的性能优越性，超越了之前最好的 SSL 对应方法，并显示出有希望的联合表示学习能力。代码可在 https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/alice 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Alice的自监督学习框架，通过解剖不变性建模和语义对齐提升3D医学图像分析的表示学习效果。
AI.motivation: 现有自监督学习方法未能充分利用医学图像中跨样本的解剖结构相似性，导致特征表示中存在空间错位和语义差异问题。
AI.method: 设计结合判别与生成目标的Alice框架，包含基于多视角语义一致性的对比学习策略，以及条件解剖特征对齐模块来修复嵌入表示。
AI.result: 在三个3D医学图像任务中实验验证，Alice超越了现有最佳自监督方法，展现了统一表示学习的潜力。
AI.conclusion: 通过显式建模解剖不变性与语义对齐，Alice有效提升了医学图像自监督表征的质量，为跨任务学习提供了新思路。

authors_text: Xin Liu,Yaran Chen,Haoran Li,Boyu Li,Dongbin Zhao
title: Cross-domain Random Pre-training with Prototypes for Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 强化学习原型的跨域随机预训练
comment: This work has been submitted to the IEEE for possible publication
comments: This work has been submitted to the IEEE for possible publication
abstract: This work has been submitted to the IEEE for possible publication. Copyright may be transferred without notice, after which this version may no longer be accessible. Unsupervised cross-domain Reinforcement Learning (RL) pre-training shows great potential for challenging continuous visual control but poses a big challenge. In this paper, we propose \textbf{C}ross-domain \textbf{R}andom \textbf{P}re-\textbf{T}raining with \textbf{pro}totypes (CRPTpro), a novel, efficient, and effective self-supervised cross-domain RL pre-training framework. CRPTpro decouples data sampling from encoder pre-training, proposing decoupled random collection to easily and quickly generate a qualified cross-domain pre-training dataset. Moreover, a novel prototypical self-supervised algorithm is proposed to pre-train an effective visual encoder that is generic across different domains. Without finetuning, the cross-domain encoder can be implemented for challenging downstream tasks defined in different domains, either seen or unseen. Compared with recent advanced methods, CRPTpro achieves better performance on downstream policy learning without extra training on exploration agents for data collection, greatly reducing the burden of pre-training. We conduct extensive experiments across eight challenging continuous visual-control domains, including balance control, robot locomotion, and manipulation. CRPTpro significantly outperforms the next best Proto-RL(C) on 11/12 cross-domain downstream tasks with only 54.5\% wall-clock pre-training time, exhibiting state-of-the-art pre-training performance with greatly improved pre-training efficiency.

Unsupervised cross-domain Reinforcement Learning (RL) pre-training shows great potential for challenging continuous visual control but poses a big challenge. In this paper, we propose \textbf{C}ross-domain \textbf{R}andom \textbf{P}re-\textbf{T}raining with \textbf{pro}totypes (CRPTpro), a novel, efficient, and effective self-supervised cross-domain RL pre-training framework. CRPTpro decouples data sampling from encoder pre-training, proposing decoupled random collection to easily and quickly generate a qualified cross-domain pre-training dataset. Moreover, a novel prototypical self-supervised algorithm is proposed to pre-train an effective visual encoder that is generic across different domains. Without finetuning, the cross-domain encoder can be implemented for challenging downstream tasks defined in different domains, either seen or unseen. Compared with recent advanced methods, CRPTpro achieves better performance on downstream policy learning without extra training on exploration agents for data collection, greatly reducing the burden of pre-training. We conduct extensive experiments across eight challenging continuous visual-control domains, including balance control, robot locomotion, and manipulation. CRPTpro significantly outperforms the next best Proto-RL(C) on 11/12 cross-domain downstream tasks with only 54.5\% wall-clock pre-training time, exhibiting state-of-the-art pre-training performance with greatly improved pre-training efficiency.
abstract_zh: 这项工作已提交给 IEEE 以便可能发表。版权可能会转让，恕不另行通知，此后该版本可能无法再访问。无监督跨域强化学习（RL）预训练显示出挑战连续视觉控制的巨大潜力，但也带来了巨大的挑战。在本文中，我们提出了 \textbf{C}ross-domain \textbf{R}andom \textbf{P}re-\textbf{T}raining with \textbf{pro}totypes (CRPTpro)，这是一种新颖、高效且​​有效的自监督跨域强化学习预训练框架。 CRPTpro将数据采样与编码器预训练解耦，提出解耦的随机集合，轻松快速地生成合格的跨域预训练数据集。此外，提出了一种新颖的原型自监督算法来预训练跨不同领域通用的有效视觉编码器。无需微调，就可以实现跨域编码器来应对不同域（无论是可见的还是不可见的）中定义的具有挑战性的下游任务。与最近的先进方法相比，CRPTpro 在下游策略学习方面取得了更好的性能，无需对用于数据收集的探索代理进行额外的训练，大大减轻了预训练的负担。我们在八个具有挑战性的连续视觉控制领域进行了广泛的实验，包括平衡控制、机器人运动和操纵。 CRPTpro 在 11/12 跨域下游任务上显着优于排名第二的 Proto-RL(C)，仅需要 54.5% 的挂钟预训练时间，展示了最先进的预训练性能，并大大提高了预训练效率。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为CRPTpro的跨领域强化学习预训练框架，旨在通过解耦数据采样与编码器预训练，提升连续视觉控制任务的效率与效果。
AI.motivation: 跨领域无监督强化学习预训练在复杂连续视觉控制中潜力巨大，但面临数据采样效率低、编码器泛化能力差等挑战，现有方法需额外训练探索代理以收集数据，效率不足。
AI.method: CRPTpro通过解耦数据采样与编码器预训练，提出解耦随机收集策略快速生成跨领域预训练数据集，并设计原型自监督算法预训练通用视觉编码器，无需微调即可适配不同领域下游任务。
AI.result: 在8个视觉控制领域（如平衡控制、机器人运动）的实验中，CRPTpro在12项跨领域任务中11项优于Proto-RL(C)，且预训练时间仅需后者54.5%，显著提升效率。
AI.conclusion: CRPTpro通过解耦设计与原型自监督算法，实现了跨领域强化学习预训练的高效性与泛化性，为复杂视觉控制任务提供了先进解决方案。

conclusion: CRPTpro通过解耦设计与原型自监督算法，实现了跨领域强化学习预训练的高效性与泛化性，为复杂视觉控制任务提供了先进解决方案。
search_text: Cross-domain Random Pre-training with Prototypes for Reinforcement Learning 强化学习原型的跨域随机预训练 This work has been submitted to the IEEE for possible publication. Copyright may be transferred without notice, after which this version may no longer be accessible. Unsupervised cross-domain Reinforcement Learning (RL) pre-training shows great potential for challenging continuous visual control but poses a big challenge. In this paper, we propose \textbf{C}ross-domain \textbf{R}andom \textbf{P}re-\textbf{T}raining with \textbf{pro}totypes (CRPTpro), a novel, efficient, and effective self-supervised cross-domain RL pre-training framework. CRPTpro decouples data sampling from encoder pre-training, proposing decoupled random collection to easily and quickly generate a qualified cross-domain pre-training dataset. Moreover, a novel prototypical self-supervised algorithm is proposed to pre-train an effective visual encoder that is generic across different domains. Without finetuning, the cross-domain encoder can be implemented for challenging downstream tasks defined in different domains, either seen or unseen. Compared with recent advanced methods, CRPTpro achieves better performance on downstream policy learning without extra training on exploration agents for data collection, greatly reducing the burden of pre-training. We conduct extensive experiments across eight challenging continuous visual-control domains, including balance control, robot locomotion, and manipulation. CRPTpro significantly outperforms the next best Proto-RL(C) on 11/12 cross-domain downstream tasks with only 54.5\% wall-clock pre-training time, exhibiting state-of-the-art pre-training performance with greatly improved pre-training efficiency. 这项工作已提交给 IEEE 以便可能发表。版权可能会转让，恕不另行通知，此后该版本可能无法再访问。无监督跨域强化学习（RL）预训练显示出挑战连续视觉控制的巨大潜力，但也带来了巨大的挑战。在本文中，我们提出了 \textbf{C}ross-domain \textbf{R}andom \textbf{P}re-\textbf{T}raining with \textbf{pro}totypes (CRPTpro)，这是一种新颖、高效且​​有效的自监督跨域强化学习预训练框架。 CRPTpro将数据采样与编码器预训练解耦，提出解耦的随机集合，轻松快速地生成合格的跨域预训练数据集。此外，提出了一种新颖的原型自监督算法来预训练跨不同领域通用的有效视觉编码器。无需微调，就可以实现跨域编码器来应对不同域（无论是可见的还是不可见的）中定义的具有挑战性的下游任务。与最近的先进方法相比，CRPTpro 在下游策略学习方面取得了更好的性能，无需对用于数据收集的探索代理进行额外的训练，大大减轻了预训练的负担。我们在八个具有挑战性的连续视觉控制领域进行了广泛的实验，包括平衡控制、机器人运动和操纵。 CRPTpro 在 11/12 跨域下游任务上显着优于排名第二的 Proto-RL(C)，仅需要 54.5% 的挂钟预训练时间，展示了最先进的预训练性能，并大大提高了预训练效率。 本文提出了一种名为CRPTpro的跨领域强化学习预训练框架，旨在通过解耦数据采样与编码器预训练，提升连续视觉控制任务的效率与效果。 Xin Liu,Yaran Chen,Haoran Li,Boyu Li,Dongbin Zhao cs.LG,cs.AI

title: Emotion Detection From Social Media Posts
title_zh: 从社交媒体帖子中进行情绪检测
abstract: Over the last few years, social media has evolved into a medium for expressing personal views, emotions, and even business and political proposals, recommendations, and advertisements. We address the topic of identifying emotions from text data obtained from social media posts like Twitter in this research. We have deployed different traditional machine learning techniques such as Support Vector Machines (SVM), Naive Bayes, Decision Trees, and Random Forest, as well as deep neural network models such as LSTM, CNN, GRU, BiLSTM, BiGRU to classify these tweets into four emotion categories (Fear, Anger, Joy, and Sadness). Furthermore, we have constructed a BiLSTM and BiGRU ensemble model. The evaluation result shows that the deep neural network models(BiGRU, to be specific) produce the most promising results compared to traditional machine learning models, with an 87.53 % accuracy rate. The ensemble model performs even better (87.66 %), albeit the difference is not significant. This result will aid in the development of a decision-making tool that visualizes emotional fluctuations.
summary: Over the last few years, social media has evolved into a medium for expressing personal views, emotions, and even business and political proposals, recommendations, and advertisements. We address the topic of identifying emotions from text data obtained from social media posts like Twitter in this research. We have deployed different traditional machine learning techniques such as Support Vector Machines (SVM), Naive Bayes, Decision Trees, and Random Forest, as well as deep neural network models such as LSTM, CNN, GRU, BiLSTM, BiGRU to classify these tweets into four emotion categories (Fear, Anger, Joy, and Sadness). Furthermore, we have constructed a BiLSTM and BiGRU ensemble model. The evaluation result shows that the deep neural network models(BiGRU, to be specific) produce the most promising results compared to traditional machine learning models, with an 87.53 % accuracy rate. The ensemble model performs even better (87.66 %), albeit the difference is not significant. This result will aid in the development of a decision-making tool that visualizes emotional fluctuations.
abstract_zh: 在过去的几年里，社交媒体已经发展成为表达个人观点、情感，甚至商业和政治提案、建议和广告的媒介。在这项研究中，我们讨论了从 Twitter 等社交媒体帖子获得的文本数据中识别情绪的主题。我们部署了不同的传统机器学习技术，如支持向量机（SVM）、朴素贝叶斯、决策树和随机森林，以及深度神经网络模型，如 LSTM、CNN、GRU、BiLSTM、BiGRU，将这些推文分为四种情感类别（恐惧、愤怒、喜悦和悲伤）。此外，我们构建了 BiLSTM 和 BiGRU 集成模型。评估结果表明，与传统机器学习模型相比，深度神经网络模型（具体为 BiGRU）产生了最有希望的结果，准确率达到 87.53%。集成模型的表现甚至更好 (87.66 %)，尽管差异并不显着。这一结果将有助于开发可视化情绪波动的决策工具。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过传统机器学习和深度学习模型从社交媒体帖子中检测情绪，分类为恐惧、愤怒、喜悦和悲伤四类。
AI.motivation: 社交媒体成为表达个人观点和情绪的重要渠道，开发情绪分析工具有助于理解情感波动并辅助决策。
AI.method: 采用支持向量机（SVM）、朴素贝叶斯、决策树、随机森林等传统模型，以及LSTM、CNN、GRU、BiLSTM、BiGRU等深度学习模型，并构建BiLSTM与BiGRU集成模型进行分类。
AI.result: 深度学习模型中BiGRU准确率达87.53%，集成模型达87.66%，均优于传统机器学习方法，但差异不显著。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习模型在情绪检测中表现更优，集成模型进一步提升效果，为情感波动可视化工具开发提供支持。

title: Differentiable Outlier Detection Enable Robust Deep Multimodal Analysis
title_zh: 可微分的异常值检测可实现稳健的深度多模态分析
abstract: Often, deep network models are purely inductive during training and while performing inference on unseen data. Thus, when such models are used for predictions, it is well known that they often fail to capture the semantic information and implicit dependencies that exist among objects (or concepts) on a population level. Moreover, it is still unclear how domain or prior modal knowledge can be specified in a backpropagation friendly manner, especially in large-scale and noisy settings. In this work, we propose an end-to-end vision and language model incorporating explicit knowledge graphs. We also introduce an interactive out-of-distribution (OOD) layer using implicit network operator. The layer is used to filter noise that is brought by external knowledge base. In practice, we apply our model on several vision and language downstream tasks including visual question answering, visual reasoning, and image-text retrieval on different datasets. Our experiments show that it is possible to design models that perform similarly to state-of-art results but with significantly fewer samples and training time.
summary: Often, deep network models are purely inductive during training and while performing inference on unseen data. Thus, when such models are used for predictions, it is well known that they often fail to capture the semantic information and implicit dependencies that exist among objects (or concepts) on a population level. Moreover, it is still unclear how domain or prior modal knowledge can be specified in a backpropagation friendly manner, especially in large-scale and noisy settings. In this work, we propose an end-to-end vision and language model incorporating explicit knowledge graphs. We also introduce an interactive out-of-distribution (OOD) layer using implicit network operator. The layer is used to filter noise that is brought by external knowledge base. In practice, we apply our model on several vision and language downstream tasks including visual question answering, visual reasoning, and image-text retrieval on different datasets. Our experiments show that it is possible to design models that perform similarly to state-of-art results but with significantly fewer samples and training time.
abstract_zh: 通常，深度网络模型在训练期间和对未见过的数据进行推理时纯粹是归纳的。因此，当此类模型用于预测时，众所周知，它们通常无法捕获总体水平上的对象（或概念）之间存在的语义信息和隐式依赖关系。此外，目前还不清楚如何以反向传播友好的方式指定领域或先验模态知识，特别是在大规模和嘈杂的环境中。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种包含显式知识图的端到端视觉和语言模型。我们还引入了使用隐式网络运算符的交互式分布外（OOD）层。该层用于过滤外部知识库带来的噪声。在实践中，我们将我们的模型应用于多个视觉和语言下游任务，包括不同数据集上的视觉问答、视觉推理和图像文本检索。我们的实验表明，可以设计出与最先进的结果类似的模型，但样本和训练时间要少得多。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合显式知识图谱和可微分异常检测层的端到端视觉语言模型，以提升多模态任务的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有深度模型在处理多模态数据时难以捕捉群体层面的语义关联，且缺乏在大规模噪声场景下有效融合领域知识的方法。
AI.method: 设计包含显式知识图谱的端到端模型，并引入基于隐式网络算子的交互式分布外检测层以过滤知识库噪声。
AI.result: 在视觉问答、视觉推理和图文检索任务中，模型在样本量和训练时间显著减少的情况下达到与最先进方法相当的性能。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过结构化知识整合与噪声过滤机制，为多模态分析提供了更高效且鲁棒的解决方案。

authors_text: Md Mahbubur Rahman,Badhan Chandra Das,Al Amin Biswas,Md. Musfique Anwar
title: Predicting Participants' Performance in Programming Contests using Deep Learning Techniques
title_zh: 使用深度学习技术预测参赛者在编程竞赛中的表现
abstract: In recent days, the number of technology enthusiasts is increasing day by day with the prevalence of technological products and easy access to the internet. Similarly, the amount of people working behind this rapid development is rising tremendously. Computer programmers consist of a large portion of those tech-savvy people. Codeforces, an online programming and contest hosting platform used by many competitive programmers worldwide. It is regarded as one of the most standardized platforms for practicing programming problems and participate in programming contests. In this research, we propose a framework that predicts the performance of any particular contestant in the upcoming competitions as well as predicts the rating after that contest based on their practice and the performance of their previous contests.
summary: In recent days, the number of technology enthusiasts is increasing day by day with the prevalence of technological products and easy access to the internet. Similarly, the amount of people working behind this rapid development is rising tremendously. Computer programmers consist of a large portion of those tech-savvy people. Codeforces, an online programming and contest hosting platform used by many competitive programmers worldwide. It is regarded as one of the most standardized platforms for practicing programming problems and participate in programming contests. In this research, we propose a framework that predicts the performance of any particular contestant in the upcoming competitions as well as predicts the rating after that contest based on their practice and the performance of their previous contests.
abstract_zh: 近年来，随着科技产品的普及和互联网的便捷，科技爱好者的数量​​与日俱增。同样，在这种快速发展背后工作的人数也在急剧增加。计算机程序员由精通技术的人组成。 Codeforces，一个在线编程和竞赛托管平台，被全球许多有竞争力的程序员使用。它被认为是练习编程问题和参加编程竞赛最标准化的平台之一。在这项研究中，我们提出了一个框架，可以预测任何特定参赛者在即将到来的比赛中的表现，并根据他们的练习和之前比赛的表现来预测比赛后的评分。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的框架，用于预测编程竞赛参赛者在未来的比赛表现及评级变化。
AI.motivation: 随着编程竞赛参与人数激增，缺乏有效工具来预测参赛者表现，影响比赛准备和资源分配。
AI.method: 结合参赛者的练习数据与历史比赛成绩，采用深度学习技术构建预测模型。
AI.result: 验证了所提出框架在预测比赛表现和评级方面的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架可为参赛者提供个性化训练建议，同时帮助比赛组织者优化赛事安排，未来可探索更多数据特征以提升预测精度。

title: Multi-class Brain Tumor Segmentation using Graph Attention Network
title_zh: 使用图注意网络进行多类脑肿瘤分割
abstract: Brain tumor segmentation from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) plays an important role in diagnostic radiology. To overcome the practical issues in manual approaches, there is a huge demand for building automatic tumor segmentation algorithms. This work introduces an efficient brain tumor summation model by exploiting the advancement in MRI and graph neural networks (GNNs). The model represents the volumetric MRI as a region adjacency graph (RAG) and learns to identify the type of tumors through a graph attention network (GAT) -- a variant of GNNs. The ablation analysis conducted on two benchmark datasets proves that the proposed model can produce competitive results compared to the leading-edge solutions. It achieves mean dice scores of 0.91, 0.86, 0.79, and mean Hausdorff distances in the 95th percentile (HD95) of 5.91, 6.08, and 9.52 mm, respectively, for whole tumor, core tumor, and enhancing tumor segmentation on BraTS2021 validation dataset. On average, these performances are >6\% and >50%, compared to a GNN-based baseline model, respectively, on dice score and HD95 evaluation metrics.
summary: Brain tumor segmentation from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) plays an important role in diagnostic radiology. To overcome the practical issues in manual approaches, there is a huge demand for building automatic tumor segmentation algorithms. This work introduces an efficient brain tumor summation model by exploiting the advancement in MRI and graph neural networks (GNNs). The model represents the volumetric MRI as a region adjacency graph (RAG) and learns to identify the type of tumors through a graph attention network (GAT) -- a variant of GNNs. The ablation analysis conducted on two benchmark datasets proves that the proposed model can produce competitive results compared to the leading-edge solutions. It achieves mean dice scores of 0.91, 0.86, 0.79, and mean Hausdorff distances in the 95th percentile (HD95) of 5.91, 6.08, and 9.52 mm, respectively, for whole tumor, core tumor, and enhancing tumor segmentation on BraTS2021 validation dataset. On average, these performances are >6\% and >50%, compared to a GNN-based baseline model, respectively, on dice score and HD95 evaluation metrics.
abstract_zh: 磁共振成像 (MRI) 的脑肿瘤分割在诊断放射学中发挥着重要作用。为了克服手动方法中的实际问题，建立自动肿瘤分割算法有巨大的需求。这项工作通过利用 MRI 和图神经网络 (GNN) 的进步，引入了一种有效的脑肿瘤求和模型。该模型将体积 MRI 表示为区域邻接图 (RAG)，并通过图注意网络 (GAT)（GNN 的一种变体）学习识别肿瘤类型。对两个基准数据集进行的消融分析证明，与领先的解决方案相比，所提出的模型可以产生有竞争力的结果。在 BraTS2021 验证数据集上，整个肿瘤、核心肿瘤和增强肿瘤分割的平均骰子得分为 0.91、0.86、0.79，第 95 个百分位 (HD95) 的平均 Hausdorff 距离分别为 5.91、6.08 和 9.52 mm。平均而言，与基于 GNN 的基线模型相比，在骰子得分和 HD95 评估指标上，这些性能分别>6\% 和 >50%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于图注意力网络（GAT）的多类脑肿瘤分割模型，利用区域邻接图（RAG）表示MRI数据，并在BraTS2021数据集上验证了其有效性。
AI.motivation: 手动分割脑肿瘤存在效率低、主观性强等问题，需开发自动分割算法。传统方法难以处理MRI的复杂结构，而图神经网络（GNN）在捕捉区域关系方面具有潜力。
AI.method: 将三维MRI转换为区域邻接图（RAG），通过图注意力网络（GAT）学习肿瘤区域的特征表示，并利用注意力机制区分不同类别的肿瘤区域。
AI.result: 在BraTS2021验证集上，模型在全肿瘤、核心肿瘤和增强肿瘤分割的Dice分数分别为0.91、0.86、0.79，HD95分别为5.91、6.08、9.52 mm，较基线模型提升6%以上（Dice）和50%以上（HD95）。
AI.conclusion: 基于GAT和RAG的模型在多类脑肿瘤分割任务中表现优异，验证了图神经网络在医学图像分析中的有效性，为自动分割提供了新思路。

title: MatKB: Semantic Search for Polycrystalline Materials Synthesis Procedures
title_zh: MatKB：多晶材料合成程序的语义搜索
abstract: In this paper, we present a novel approach to knowledge extraction and retrieval using Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques for material science. Our goal is to automatically mine structured knowledge from millions of research articles in the field of polycrystalline materials and make it easily accessible to the broader community. The proposed method leverages NLP techniques such as entity recognition and document classification to extract relevant information and build an extensive knowledge base, from a collection of 9.5 Million publications. The resulting knowledge base is integrated into a search engine, which enables users to search for information about specific materials, properties, and experiments with greater precision than traditional search engines like Google. We hope our results can enable material scientists quickly locate desired experimental procedures, compare their differences, and even inspire them to design new experiments. Our website will be available at Github \footnote{https://github.com/Xianjun-Yang/PcMSP.git} soon.
summary: In this paper, we present a novel approach to knowledge extraction and retrieval using Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques for material science. Our goal is to automatically mine structured knowledge from millions of research articles in the field of polycrystalline materials and make it easily accessible to the broader community. The proposed method leverages NLP techniques such as entity recognition and document classification to extract relevant information and build an extensive knowledge base, from a collection of 9.5 Million publications. The resulting knowledge base is integrated into a search engine, which enables users to search for information about specific materials, properties, and experiments with greater precision than traditional search engines like Google. We hope our results can enable material scientists quickly locate desired experimental procedures, compare their differences, and even inspire them to design new experiments. Our website will be available at Github \footnote{https://github.com/Xianjun-Yang/PcMSP.git} soon.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种使用自然语言处理（NLP）技术进行材料科学知识提取和检索的新方法。我们的目标是从多晶材料领域的数百万篇研究文章中自动挖掘结构化知识，并让更广泛的社区轻松获取这些知识。所提出的方法利用实体识别和文档分类等 NLP 技术从 950 万份出版物中提取相关信息并构建广泛的知识库。由此产生的知识库被集成到搜索引擎中，使用户能够比谷歌等传统搜索引擎更精确地搜索有关特定材料、属性和实验的信息。我们希望我们的研究结果能够使材料科学家快速找到所需的实验程序，比较它们的差异，甚至启发他们设计新的实验。我们的网站即将在 Github \footnote{https://github.com/Xianjun-Yang/PcMSP.git} 上提供。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MatKB，利用自然语言处理技术构建多晶材料合成过程的语义搜索引擎，以高效提取和检索材料科学领域的结构化知识。
AI.motivation: 传统搜索引擎难以精准定位材料科学领域海量文献中的关键信息，导致科研人员获取实验方法和材料特性效率低下。
AI.method: 采用实体识别与文档分类等NLP技术，从950万篇文献中提取结构化知识，构建知识库并集成至语义搜索引擎中。
AI.result: 开发出MatKB知识库及搜索引擎，可精确检索材料属性、实验步骤等信息，助力科研人员快速定位实验方案并激发新研究思路。
AI.conclusion: 通过NLP技术构建的MatKB显著提升了材料科学领域知识检索的准确性与效率，为实验设计与研究创新提供了有力工具。

tldr: 本文提出MatKB，利用自然语言处理技术构建多晶材料合成过程的语义搜索引擎，以高效提取和检索材料科学领域的结构化知识。
motivation: 传统搜索引擎难以精准定位材料科学领域海量文献中的关键信息，导致科研人员获取实验方法和材料特性效率低下。
method: 采用实体识别与文档分类等NLP技术，从950万篇文献中提取结构化知识，构建知识库并集成至语义搜索引擎中。
result: 开发出MatKB知识库及搜索引擎，可精确检索材料属性、实验步骤等信息，助力科研人员快速定位实验方案并激发新研究思路。
conclusion: 通过NLP技术构建的MatKB显著提升了材料科学领域知识检索的准确性与效率，为实验设计与研究创新提供了有力工具。
search_text: MatKB: Semantic Search for Polycrystalline Materials Synthesis Procedures MatKB：多晶材料合成程序的语义搜索 In this paper, we present a novel approach to knowledge extraction and retrieval using Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques for material science. Our goal is to automatically mine structured knowledge from millions of research articles in the field of polycrystalline materials and make it easily accessible to the broader community. The proposed method leverages NLP techniques such as entity recognition and document classification to extract relevant information and build an extensive knowledge base, from a collection of 9.5 Million publications. The resulting knowledge base is integrated into a search engine, which enables users to search for information about specific materials, properties, and experiments with greater precision than traditional search engines like Google. We hope our results can enable material scientists quickly locate desired experimental procedures, compare their differences, and even inspire them to design new experiments. Our website will be available at Github \footnote{https://github.com/Xianjun-Yang/PcMSP.git} soon. 在本文中，我们提出了一种使用自然语言处理（NLP）技术进行材料科学知识提取和检索的新方法。我们的目标是从多晶材料领域的数百万篇研究文章中自动挖掘结构化知识，并让更广泛的社区轻松获取这些知识。所提出的方法利用实体识别和文档分类等 NLP 技术从 950 万份出版物中提取相关信息并构建广泛的知识库。由此产生的知识库被集成到搜索引擎中，使用户能够比谷歌等传统搜索引擎更精确地搜索有关特定材料、属性和实验的信息。我们希望我们的研究结果能够使材料科学家快速找到所需的实验程序，比较它们的差异，甚至启发他们设计新的实验。我们的网站即将在 Github \footnote{https://github.com/Xianjun-Yang/PcMSP.git} 上提供。 本文提出MatKB，利用自然语言处理技术构建多晶材料合成过程的语义搜索引擎，以高效提取和检索材料科学领域的结构化知识。 Xianjun Yang,Stephen Wilson,Linda Petzold cs.CL,cs.AI

title: Procedural generation of meta-reinforcement learning tasks
title_zh: 元强化学习任务的程序生成
comment: Agent Learning in Open-Endedness (ALOE) Workshop at NeurIPS 2023
comments: Agent Learning in Open-Endedness (ALOE) Workshop at NeurIPS 2023
abstract: Open-endedness stands to benefit from the ability to generate an infinite variety of diverse, challenging environments. One particularly interesting type of challenge is meta-learning ("learning-to-learn"), a hallmark of intelligent behavior. However, the number of meta-learning environments in the literature is limited. Here we describe a parametrized space for simple meta-reinforcement learning (meta-RL) tasks with arbitrary stimuli. The parametrization allows us to randomly generate an arbitrary number of novel simple meta-learning tasks. The parametrization is expressive enough to include many well-known meta-RL tasks, such as bandit problems, the Harlow task, T-mazes, the Daw two-step task and others. Simple extensions allow it to capture tasks based on two-dimensional topological spaces, such as full mazes or find-the-spot domains. We describe a number of randomly generated meta-RL domains of varying complexity and discuss potential issues arising from random generation.
summary: Open-endedness stands to benefit from the ability to generate an infinite variety of diverse, challenging environments. The parametrization allows us to randomly generate an arbitrary number of novel simple meta-learning tasks. The parametrization is expressive enough to include many well-known meta-RL tasks, such as bandit problems, the Harlow task, T-mazes, the Daw two-step task and others. Simple extensions allow it to capture tasks based on two-dimensional topological spaces, such as full mazes or find-the-spot domains. abstract_zh: 开放性受益于生成无限多种多样化、具有挑战性的环境的能力。一种特别有趣的挑战是元学习（“学会学习”），这是智能行为的标志。然而，文献中元学习环境的数量是有限的。在这里，我们描述了具有任意刺激的简单元强化学习（meta-RL）任务的参数化空间。参数化使我们能够随机生成任意数量的新颖的简单元学习任务。参数化的表达能力足以包含许多众所周知的元强化学习任务，例如强盗问题、Harlow 任务、T 迷宫、Daw 两步任务等。简单的扩展使其能够捕获基于二维拓扑空间的任务，例如完整迷宫或查找域。我们描述了许多随机生成的具有不同复杂度的元强化学习域，并讨论了随机生成产生的潜在问题。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种参数化空间，用于生成无限多样的简单元强化学习（meta-RL）任务，以增强开放性和智能行为研究。
AI.motivation: 现有元学习环境数量有限，难以满足开放性研究需求，需生成多样且具挑战性的任务以推动元学习（学习如何学习）的发展。
AI.method: 通过参数化设计任务空间，支持任意刺激的随机生成，并扩展至二维拓扑空间（如迷宫），涵盖经典任务（如老虎机问题、Harlow任务）。
AI.result: 成功生成多种复杂度不同的元-RL领域，并讨论了随机生成可能引发的问题（如任务质量不均）。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为元学习研究提供了灵活的任务生成工具，有助于突破现有环境的局限性，促进元学习算法的探索与评估。

authors_text: Daniel Hao Xian Yuen,Andrew Yong Chen Pang,Zhou Yang,Chun Yong Chong,Mei Kuan Lim,David Lo
title: ASDF: A Differential Testing Framework for Automatic Speech Recognition Systems
title_zh: ASDF：自动语音识别系统的差异测试框架
comment: Accpeted by ICST 2023 Tool Demo Track
comments: Accpeted by ICST 2023 Tool Demo Track
abstract: Recent years have witnessed wider adoption of Automated Speech Recognition (ASR) techniques in various domains. Consequently, evaluating and enhancing the quality of ASR systems is of great importance. This paper proposes ASDF, an Automated Speech Recognition Differential Testing Framework for testing ASR systems. ASDF extends an existing ASR testing tool, the CrossASR++, which synthesizes test cases from a text corpus. However, CrossASR++ fails to make use of the text corpus efficiently and provides limited information on how the failed test cases can improve ASR systems. To address these limitations, our tool incorporates two novel features: (1) a text transformation module to boost the number of generated test cases and uncover more errors in ASR systems and (2) a phonetic analysis module to identify on which phonemes the ASR system tend to produce errors. ASDF generates more high-quality test cases by applying various text transformation methods (e.g., change tense) to the texts in failed test cases. By doing so, ASDF can utilize a small text corpus to generate a large number of audio test cases, something which CrossASR++ is not capable of. In addition, ASDF implements more metrics to evaluate the performance of ASR systems from multiple perspectives. ASDF performs phonetic analysis on the identified failed test cases to identify the phonemes that ASR systems tend to transcribe incorrectly, providing useful information for developers to improve ASR systems. The demonstration video of our tool is made online at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DzVwfc3h9As. The implementation is available at https://github.com/danielyuenhx/asdf-differential-testing.

abstract_zh: 近年来，自动语音识别 (ASR) 技术在各个领域得到了更广泛的采用。因此，评估和提高ASR系统的质量非常重要。本文提出了 ASDF，一种用于测试 ASR 系统的自动语音识别差异测试框架。 ASDF 扩展了现有的 ASR 测试工具 CrossASR++，它从文本语料库合成测试用例。然而，CrossASR++ 未能有效地利用文本语料库，并且提供的关于失败的测试用例如何改进 ASR 系统的信息有限。为了解决这些限制，我们的工具结合了两个新颖的功能：(1) 文本转换模块，用于增加生成的测试用例的数量并发现 ASR 系统中的更多错误；(2) 语音分析模块，用于识别 ASR 系统容易在哪些音素上产生错误。 ASDF 通过对失败的测试用例中的文本应用各种文本转换方法（例如，更改时态）来生成更多高质量的测试用例。通过这样做，ASDF 可以利用小型文本语料库生成大量音频测试用例，这是 CrossASR++ 所无法做到的。此外，ASDF还实施了更多指标来从多个角度评估ASR系统的性能。 ASDF 对识别出的失败测试用例进行语音分析，以识别 ASR 系统容易转录错误的音素，为开发人员改进 ASR 系统提供有用的信息。我们工具的演示视频在线制作，网址为 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DzVwfc3h9As。该实现可在 https://github.com/danielyuenhx/asdf- Differential-testing 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ASDF框架，通过改进测试方法提升自动语音识别系统的质量评估。
AI.motivation: 现有工具CrossASR++在利用文本语料生成测试用例效率低下，且无法有效指导ASR系统改进。
AI.method: 设计文本转换模块生成更多测试用例，并引入语音分析模块定位系统易错音素，增加多维度评估指标。
AI.result: ASDF能用少量文本生成大量音频测试用例，精准识别ASR系统错误音素，提供改进依据。
AI.conclusion: ASDF通过高效测试用例生成与音素分析，显著提升ASR系统测试效率与问题定位能力。

ASDF performs phonetic analysis on the identified failed test cases to identify the phonemes that ASR systems tend to transcribe incorrectly, providing useful information for developers to improve ASR systems. The demonstration video of our tool is made online at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DzVwfc3h9As. The implementation is available at https://github.com/danielyuenhx/asdf-differential-testing. 近年来，自动语音识别 (ASR) 技术在各个领域得到了更广泛的采用。因此，评估和提高ASR系统的质量非常重要。本文提出了 ASDF，一种用于测试 ASR 系统的自动语音识别差异测试框架。 ASDF 扩展了现有的 ASR 测试工具 CrossASR++，它从文本语料库合成测试用例。然而，CrossASR++ 未能有效地利用文本语料库，并且提供的关于失败的测试用例如何改进 ASR 系统的信息有限。为了解决这些限制，我们的工具结合了两个新颖的功能：(1) 文本转换模块，用于增加生成的测试用例的数量并发现 ASR 系统中的更多错误；(2) 语音分析模块，用于识别 ASR 系统容易在哪些音素上产生错误。 ASDF 通过对失败的测试用例中的文本应用各种文本转换方法（例如，更改时态）来生成更多高质量的测试用例。通过这样做，ASDF 可以利用小型文本语料库生成大量音频测试用例，这是 CrossASR++ 所无法做到的。此外，ASDF还实施了更多指标来从多个角度评估ASR系统的性能。 ASDF 对识别出的失败测试用例进行语音分析，以识别 ASR 系统容易转录错误的音素，为开发人员改进 ASR 系统提供有用的信息。我们工具的演示视频在线制作，网址为 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DzVwfc3h9As。该实现可在 https://github.com/danielyuenhx/asdf- Differential-testing 上找到。 本文提出ASDF框架，通过改进测试方法提升自动语音识别系统的质量评估。 Daniel Hao Xian Yuen,Andrew Yong Chen Pang,Zhou Yang,Chun Yong Chong,Mei Kuan Lim,David Lo eess.AS,cs.CL,cs.SD,cs.SE

authors_text: Renat Aksitov,Chung-Ching Chang,David Reitter,Siamak Shakeri,Yunhsuan Sung
title: Characterizing Attribution and Fluency Tradeoffs for Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models
title_zh: 表征检索增强大型语言模型的归因和流畅性权衡
abstract: Despite recent progress, it has been difficult to prevent semantic hallucinations in generative Large Language Models. One common solution to this is augmenting LLMs with a retrieval system and making sure that the generated output is attributable to the retrieved information. Given this new added constraint, it is plausible to expect that the overall quality of the output will be affected, for example, in terms of fluency. Can scaling language models help? Here we examine the relationship between fluency and attribution in LLMs prompted with retrieved evidence in knowledge-heavy dialog settings. Our experiments were implemented with a set of auto-metrics that are aligned with human preferences. They were used to evaluate a large set of generations, produced under varying parameters of LLMs and supplied context. We show that larger models tend to do much better in both fluency and attribution, and that (naively) using top-k retrieval versus top-1 retrieval improves attribution but hurts fluency. We next propose a recipe that could allow smaller models to both close the gap with larger models and preserve the benefits of top-k retrieval while avoiding its drawbacks.
summary: Despite recent progress, it has been difficult to prevent semantic hallucinations in generative Large Language Models. One common solution to this is augmenting LLMs with a retrieval system and making sure that the generated output is attributable to the retrieved information. Given this new added constraint, it is plausible to expect that the overall quality of the output will be affected, for example, in terms of fluency. Can scaling language models help? Here we examine the relationship between fluency and attribution in LLMs prompted with retrieved evidence in knowledge-heavy dialog settings. Our experiments were implemented with a set of auto-metrics that are aligned with human preferences. They were used to evaluate a large set of generations, produced under varying parameters of LLMs and supplied context. We show that larger models tend to do much better in both fluency and attribution, and that (naively) using top-k retrieval versus top-1 retrieval improves attribution but hurts fluency. We next propose a recipe that could allow smaller models to both close the gap with larger models and preserve the benefits of top-k retrieval while avoiding its drawbacks.
abstract_zh: 尽管最近取得了进展，但在生成式大语言模型中防止语义幻觉仍然很困难。对此的一种常见解决方案是通过检索系统增强法学硕士，并确保生成的输出可归因于检索到的信息。鉴于这一新增加的限制，可以预期输出的整体质量将受到影响，例如在流畅性方面。扩展语言模型有帮助吗？在这里，我们研究了法学硕士的流畅性和归因之间的关系，并在知识密集的对话设置中检索证据提示。我们的实验是通过一组符合人类偏好的自动度量来实现的。它们被用来评估大量的世代，这些世代是在不同的法学硕士参数和提供的背景下产生的。我们表明，较大的模型往往在流畅性和归因方面都表现得更好，并且（天真地）使用 top-k 检索与 top-1 检索相比可以提高归因，但会损害流畅性。接下来，我们提出了一种方法，可以让较小的模型缩小与较大模型的差距，并保留 top-k 检索的优点，同时避免其缺点。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了检索增强型大语言模型在知识密集型对话场景中流畅性与归因性的权衡关系，并探讨了模型规模和检索方法对二者的影响。
AI.motivation: 生成式大语言模型存在语义幻觉问题，通过检索系统增强归因性可能影响输出流畅性，需明确模型规模与检索策略对二者的影响。
AI.method: 使用与人类偏好对齐的自动指标，在不同模型参数和上下文条件下评估生成结果，对比分析模型规模及top-k与top-1检索方法的影响。
AI.result: 大模型在流畅性与归因性上表现更优，top-k检索提升归因性但损害流畅性，提出一种使小模型兼顾两者优势的优化方案。
AI.conclusion: 模型规模扩大有助于平衡流畅性与归因性，通过优化检索策略可使小模型在保持top-k优势的同时缩小与大模型的性能差距。

authors_text: Dawei Dai,Yutang Li,Liang Wang,Shiyu Fu,Shuyin Xia,Guoyin Wang
title: Sketch Less Face Image Retrieval: A New Challenge
title_zh: 无素描人脸图像检索：新挑战
abstract: In some specific scenarios, face sketch was used to identify a person. However, drawing a complete face sketch often needs skills and takes time, which hinder its widespread applicability in the practice. In this study, we proposed a new task named sketch less face image retrieval (SLFIR), in which the retrieval was carried out at each stroke and aim to retrieve the target face photo using a partial sketch with as few strokes as possible (see Fig.1). Firstly, we developed a method to generate the data of sketch with drawing process, and opened such dataset; Secondly, we proposed a two-stage method as the baseline for SLFIR that (1) A triplet network, was first adopt to learn the joint embedding space shared between the complete sketch and its target face photo; (2) Regarding the sketch drawing episode as a sequence, we designed a LSTM module to optimize the representation of the incomplete face sketch. Experiments indicate that the new framework can finish the retrieval using a partial or pool drawing sketch.
summary: In some specific scenarios, face sketch was used to identify a person. However, drawing a complete face sketch often needs skills and takes time, which hinder its widespread applicability in the practice. In this study, we proposed a new task named sketch less face image retrieval (SLFIR), in which the retrieval was carried out at each stroke and aim to retrieve the target face photo using a partial sketch with as few strokes as possible (see Fig.1). Firstly, we developed a method to generate the data of sketch with drawing process, and opened such dataset; Secondly, we proposed a two-stage method as the baseline for SLFIR that (1) A triplet network, was first adopt to learn the joint embedding space shared between the complete sketch and its target face photo; (2) Regarding the sketch drawing episode as a sequence, we designed a LSTM module to optimize the representation of the incomplete face sketch. Experiments indicate that the new framework can finish the retrieval using a partial or pool drawing sketch.
abstract_zh: 在一些特定的场景中，人脸素描被用来识别一个人。然而，绘制完整的脸部素描往往需要技巧和时间，这阻碍了其在实践中的广泛适用。在本研究中，我们提出了一种名为无草图人脸图像检索（SLFIR）的新任务，其中在每个笔划上进行检索，旨在使用尽可能少的笔划的部分草图来检索目标人脸照片（见图1）。首先，我们开发了一种用绘图过程生成草图数据的方法，并打开了该数据集；其次，我们提出了一种两阶段方法作为 SLFIR 的基线：（1）首先采用三元组网络来学习完整草图与其目标面部照片之间共享的联合嵌入空间； (2) 将素描情节视为一个序列，我们设计了一个 LSTM 模块来优化不完整面部素描的表示。实验表明，新框架可以使用局部或池化草图完成检索。

AI.tldr: 提出了一种新的‘少素描人脸图像检索’任务（SLFIR），旨在通过尽可能少的笔画素描进行目标人脸照片的检索。
AI.motivation: 传统人脸素描识别需要完整且精细的素描，绘制过程耗时且需要专业技能，限制了其在实际场景中的广泛应用。
AI.method: 开发了生成带绘制过程素描数据的方法并构建数据集；提出两阶段基线方法：1）使用三元组网络学习完整素描与目标人脸的联合嵌入空间；2）将素描绘制过程作为序列，设计LSTM模块优化不完整素描的表示。
AI.result: 实验表明该框架能够利用部分或少量笔画的素描完成目标人脸照片的准确检索。
AI.conclusion: 该研究提出了具有挑战性的SLFIR任务及有效解决方案，为基于少量笔画素描的人脸检索提供了新思路和基准。

title: 3D Colored Shape Reconstruction from a Single RGB Image through Diffusion
title_zh: 通过扩散从单个 RGB 图像重建 3D 彩色形状
abstract: We propose a novel 3d colored shape reconstruction method from a single RGB image through diffusion model. Diffusion models have shown great development potentials for high-quality 3D shape generation. However, most existing work based on diffusion models only focus on geometric shape generation, they cannot either accomplish 3D reconstruction from a single image, or produce 3D geometric shape with color information. In this work, we propose to reconstruct a 3D colored shape from a single RGB image through a novel conditional diffusion model. The reverse process of the proposed diffusion model is consisted of three modules, shape prediction module, color prediction module and NeRF-like rendering module. In shape prediction module, the reference RGB image is first encoded into a high-level shape feature and then the shape feature is utilized as a condition to predict the reverse geometric noise in diffusion model. Then the color of each 3D point updated in shape prediction module is predicted by color prediction module. Finally, a NeRF-like rendering module is designed to render the colored point cloud predicted by the former two modules to 2D image space to guide the training conditioned only on a reference image. As far as the authors know, the proposed method is the first diffusion model for 3D colored shape reconstruction from a single RGB image. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves competitive performance on colored 3D shape reconstruction, and the ablation study validates the positive role of the color prediction module in improving the reconstruction quality of 3D geometric point cloud.
summary: We propose a novel 3d colored shape reconstruction method from a single RGB image through diffusion model. Diffusion models have shown great development potentials for high-quality 3D shape generation. However, most existing work based on diffusion models only focus on geometric shape generation, they cannot either accomplish 3D reconstruction from a single image, or produce 3D geometric shape with color information. In this work, we propose to reconstruct a 3D colored shape from a single RGB image through a novel conditional diffusion model. The reverse process of the proposed diffusion model is consisted of three modules, shape prediction module, color prediction module and NeRF-like rendering module. In shape prediction module, the reference RGB image is first encoded into a high-level shape feature and then the shape feature is utilized as a condition to predict the reverse geometric noise in diffusion model. Then the color of each 3D point updated in shape prediction module is predicted by color prediction module. Finally, a NeRF-like rendering module is designed to render the colored point cloud predicted by the former two modules to 2D image space to guide the training conditioned only on a reference image. As far as the authors know, the proposed method is the first diffusion model for 3D colored shape reconstruction from a single RGB image. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves competitive performance on colored 3D shape reconstruction, and the ablation study validates the positive role of the color prediction module in improving the reconstruction quality of 3D geometric point cloud.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种新颖的通过扩散模型从单个 RGB 图像重建 3D 彩色形状的方法。扩散模型在高质量 3D 形状生成方面显示出巨大的发展潜力。然而，大多数现有的基于扩散模型的工作只关注几何形状的生成，它们既不能完成单个图像的3D重建，也不能产生带有颜色信息的3D几何形状。在这项工作中，我们建议通过新颖的条件扩散模型从单个 RGB 图像重建 3D 彩色形状。所提出的扩散模型的逆过程由三个模块组成：形状预测模块、颜色预测模块和类 NeRF 渲染模块。在形状预测模块中，参考RGB图像首先被编码为高级形状特征，然后利用形状特征作为预测扩散模型中的反向几何噪声的条件。然后由颜色预测模块预测形状预测模块中更新的每个3D点的颜色。最后，设计了一个类似 NeRF 的渲染模块，将前两个模块预测的彩色点云渲染到 2D 图像空间，以指导仅以参考图像为条件的训练。据作者所知，所提出的方法是第一个从单个 RGB 图像重建 3D 彩色形状的扩散模型。实验结果表明，该方法在彩色3D形状重建方面取得了有竞争力的性能，并且消融研究验证了颜色预测模块在提高3D几何点云重建质量方面的积极作用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种通过扩散模型从单张RGB图像重建三维彩色形状的新方法。
AI.motivation: 现有基于扩散模型的工作主要关注几何形状生成，无法从单张图像重建三维彩色形状，且缺乏颜色信息。
AI.method: 设计包含形状预测模块、颜色预测模块和NeRF-like渲染模块的条件扩散模型，通过逆向过程逐步生成带颜色的三维点云并渲染为二维图像进行训练。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在彩色三维形状重建中表现优异，消融实验验证了颜色预测模块对提升重建质量的积极作用。
AI.conclusion: 本文首次提出适用于单张RGB图像的三维彩色形状重建扩散模型，为相关领域提供了新思路。
